def:wff-pred.title: Well-Formed Formula
def:wff-pred.statement_preview: The set of \emph{well-formed formulas} of a first-order language \(\mathcal L\), denoted \(\mathsf{Form}_{\mathcal L}\), is the smallest set of strings satisfying: \begin{enumerate} \item every atomic formula is a well-formed formula; \item if \(\varphi\) is a well-formed formula, then \(\neg\varphi\) is a well-formed formula; \item if \(\varphi\) and \(\ps...

def:molecular.title: Molecular Formula
def:molecular.statement_preview: A \emph{molecular formula} is a well-formed formula that is not atomic. Equivalently, it is a well-formed formula whose final formation step uses a logical connective or a quantifier.
def:molecular.statement_display:
A \emph{molecular formula} is a well-formed formula that is not atomic.
Equivalently, it is a well-formed formula whose final formation step uses a
logical connective or a quantifier.
def:molecular.statement_tex:
A \emph{molecular formula} is a well-formed formula that is not atomic.
Equivalently, it is a well-formed formula whose final formation step uses a
logical connective or a quantifier.

def:scope.title: Scope of a Quantifier
def:scope.statement_preview: Let $\varphi$ be a formula of a first-order language. If $\varphi$ is of the form $\forall x\,\psi$ or $\exists x\,\psi$, then the formula $\psi$ is called the \emph{scope} of the quantifier. The quantifier is said to \emph{bind} all occurrences of the variable $x$ that appear within its scope.
def:scope.statement_display:
Let $\varphi$ be a formula of a first-order language.

If $\varphi$ is of the form $\forall x\,\psi$ or $\exists x\,\psi$, then the
formula $\psi$ is called the \emph{scope} of the quantifier.

The quantifier is said to \emph{bind} all occurrences of the variable $x$ that
appear within its scope.
def:scope.statement_tex:
Let $\varphi$ be a formula of a first-order language.

If $\varphi$ is of the form $\forall x\,\psi$ or $\exists x\,\psi$, then the
formula $\psi$ is called the \emph{scope} of the quantifier.

The quantifier is said to \emph{bind} all occurrences of the variable $x$ that
appear within its scope.

def:bound-free.title: Bound and Free Occurrences
def:bound-free.statement_preview: An occurrence of a variable $x$ in a formula $\varphi$ is \emph{bound} if it lies within the scope of a quantifier $\forall x$ or $\exists x$. An occurrence of $x$ is \emph{free} if it is not bound by any quantifier in $\varphi$.
def:bound-free.statement_display:
An occurrence of a variable $x$ in a formula $\varphi$ is \emph{bound} if it
lies within the scope of a quantifier $\forall x$ or $\exists x$.

An occurrence of $x$ is \emph{free} if it is not bound by any quantifier in
$\varphi$.
def:bound-free.statement_tex:
An occurrence of a variable $x$ in a formula $\varphi$ is \emph{bound} if it
lies within the scope of a quantifier $\forall x$ or $\exists x$.

An occurrence of $x$ is \emph{free} if it is not bound by any quantifier in
$\varphi$.

def:fv.title: Free Variables of a Formula
def:fv.statement_preview: The set $\mathrm{FV}(\varphi)$ of \emph{free variables} of a formula $\varphi$ is defined recursively: \begin{enumerate} \item If $\varphi = P(t_1,\dots,t_n)$ is atomic, then $\mathrm{FV}(\varphi) = \bigcup_{i=1}^n \mathrm{Var}(t_i)$, where $\mathrm{Var}(t_i)$ is the set of variables in term $t_i$. \item $\mathrm{FV}(\neg \varphi) = \mathrm{FV}(\varphi)$. \...
def:fv.statement_display:
The set $\mathrm{FV}(\varphi)$ of \emph{free variables} of a formula $\varphi$
is defined recursively:
\begin{enumerate}
  \item If $\varphi = P(t_1,\dots,t_n)$ is atomic, then
  $\mathrm{FV}(\varphi) = \bigcup_{i=1}^n \mathrm{Var}(t_i)$,
  where $\mathrm{Var}(t_i)$ is the set of variables in term $t_i$.

  \item $\mathrm{FV}(\neg \varphi) = \mathrm{FV}(\varphi)$.

  \item $\mathrm{FV}(\varphi \circ \psi) = \mathrm{FV}(\varphi) \cup \mathrm{FV}(\psi)$
  for any binary connective $\circ$.

  \item $\mathrm{FV}(\forall x\,\varphi) = \mathrm{FV}(\varphi) \setminus \{x\}$.

  \item $\mathrm{FV}(\exists x\,\varphi) = \mathrm{FV}(\varphi) \setminus \{x\}$.
\end{enumerate}
def:fv.statement_tex:
The set $\mathrm{FV}(\varphi)$ of \emph{free variables} of a formula $\varphi$
is defined recursively:
\begin{enumerate}
  \item If $\varphi = P(t_1,\dots,t_n)$ is atomic, then
  $\mathrm{FV}(\varphi) = \bigcup_{i=1}^n \mathrm{Var}(t_i)$,
  where $\mathrm{Var}(t_i)$ is the set of variables in term $t_i$.

  \item $\mathrm{FV}(\neg \varphi) = \mathrm{FV}(\varphi)$.

  \item $\mathrm{FV}(\varphi \circ \psi) = \mathrm{FV}(\varphi) \cup \mathrm{FV}(\psi)$
  for any binary connective $\circ$.

  \item $\mathrm{FV}(\forall x\,\varphi) = \mathrm{FV}(\varphi) \setminus \{x\}$.

  \item $\mathrm{FV}(\exists x\,\varphi) = \mathrm{FV}(\varphi) \setminus \{x\}$.
\end{enumerate}

def:sentence.title: Sentence
def:sentence.statement_preview: A \emph{sentence} (or \emph{closed formula}) is a formula with no free variables. Formally, $\varphi$ is a sentence if and only if $\mathrm{FV}(\varphi) = \varnothing$.
def:sentence.statement_display:
A \emph{sentence} (or \emph{closed formula}) is a formula with no free variables.

Formally, $\varphi$ is a sentence if and only if $\mathrm{FV}(\varphi) = \varnothing$.
def:sentence.statement_tex:
A \emph{sentence} (or \emph{closed formula}) is a formula with no free variables.

Formally, $\varphi$ is a sentence if and only if $\mathrm{FV}(\varphi) = \varnothing$.

def:subst-notation.title: Substitution Notation
def:subst-notation.statement_preview: Let $\varphi$ be a formula, $x$ a variable, and $t$ a term. The expression $\varphi[t/x]$ denotes the formula obtained from $\varphi$ by replacing every \emph{free} occurrence of $x$ with the term $t$, leaving bound occurrences of $x$ unchanged.
def:subst-notation.statement_display:
Let $\varphi$ be a formula, $x$ a variable, and $t$ a term.

The expression $\varphi[t/x]$ denotes the formula obtained from $\varphi$ by
replacing every \emph{free} occurrence of $x$ with the term $t$, leaving bound
occurrences of $x$ unchanged.
def:subst-notation.statement_tex:
Let $\varphi$ be a formula, $x$ a variable, and $t$ a term.

The expression $\varphi[t/x]$ denotes the formula obtained from $\varphi$ by
replacing every \emph{free} occurrence of $x$ with the term $t$, leaving bound
occurrences of $x$ unchanged.

def:free-for.title: Free for Substitution
def:free-for.statement_preview: A term $t$ is \emph{free for} $x$ in $\varphi$ if no free occurrence of $x$ in $\varphi$ lies within the scope of a quantifier $\forall y$ or $\exists y$ where $y$ is a variable occurring in $t$. Equivalently, $t$ is free for $x$ in $\varphi$ if the substitution $\varphi[t/x]$ does not result in any variable in $t$ becoming bound.
def:free-for.statement_display:
A term $t$ is \emph{free for} $x$ in $\varphi$ if no free occurrence of $x$ in
$\varphi$ lies within the scope of a quantifier $\forall y$ or $\exists y$
where $y$ is a variable occurring in $t$.

Equivalently, $t$ is free for $x$ in $\varphi$ if the substitution $\varphi[t/x]$
does not result in any variable in $t$ becoming bound.
def:free-for.statement_tex:
A term $t$ is \emph{free for} $x$ in $\varphi$ if no free occurrence of $x$ in
$\varphi$ lies within the scope of a quantifier $\forall y$ or $\exists y$
where $y$ is a variable occurring in $t$.

Equivalently, $t$ is free for $x$ in $\varphi$ if the substitution $\varphi[t/x]$
does not result in any variable in $t$ becoming bound.

def:alpha-equiv.title: Alpha-Equivalence
def:alpha-equiv.statement_preview: Formulas that differ only by the names of bound variables are logically equivalent. This is called \emph{alpha-equivalence} (or \emph{alphabetic variance}): \[ \forall x\,\varphi \;\equiv\; \forall y\,\varphi[y/x] \quad\text{(provided $y$ is not free in $\varphi$).} \]
def:alpha-equiv.statement_display:
Formulas that differ only by the names of bound variables are logically
equivalent. This is called \emph{alpha-equivalence} (or \emph{alphabetic
variance}):
\[
\forall x\,\varphi \;\equiv\; \forall y\,\varphi[y/x]
\quad\text{(provided $y$ is not free in $\varphi$).}
\]
def:alpha-equiv.statement_tex:
Formulas that differ only by the names of bound variables are logically
equivalent. This is called \emph{alpha-equivalence} (or \emph{alphabetic
variance}):
\[
\forall x\,\varphi \;\equiv\; \forall y\,\varphi[y/x]
\quad\text{(provided $y$ is not free in $\varphi$).}
\]

def:translation-key-predicate-logic.title: Translation Key
def:translation-key-predicate-logic.statement_preview: A \emph{translation key} is a finite record that fixes a domain of discourse and assigns intended English readings to the constant symbols, predicate symbols, and relation symbols used in a translation.
def:translation-key-predicate-logic.statement_display:
A \emph{translation key} is a finite record that fixes a domain of discourse and
assigns intended English readings to the constant symbols, predicate symbols,
and relation symbols used in a translation.
def:translation-key-predicate-logic.statement_tex:
A \emph{translation key} is a finite record that fixes a domain of discourse and
assigns intended English readings to the constant symbols, predicate symbols,
and relation symbols used in a translation.

def:domain-of-discourse-predicate-logic.title: Domain of Discourse
def:domain-of-discourse-predicate-logic.statement_preview: The \emph{domain of discourse} for a translation is the collection of objects over which the variables of the translated formulas range.
def:domain-of-discourse-predicate-logic.statement_display:
The \emph{domain of discourse} for a translation is the collection of objects
over which the variables of the translated formulas range.
def:domain-of-discourse-predicate-logic.statement_tex:
The \emph{domain of discourse} for a translation is the collection of objects
over which the variables of the translated formulas range.

def:translation-predicate-relation-symbo.title: Predicate and Relation Symbols in a Translation
def:translation-predicate-relation-symbo.statement_preview: In a translation key, a unary predicate symbol names a property of one object, and an \(n\)-ary relation symbol names a relation among \(n\) objects.
def:translation-predicate-relation-symbo.statement_display:
In a translation key, a unary predicate symbol names a property of one object,
and an \(n\)-ary relation symbol names a relation among \(n\) objects.
def:translation-predicate-relation-symbo.statement_tex:
In a translation key, a unary predicate symbol names a property of one object,
and an \(n\)-ary relation symbol names a relation among \(n\) objects.

def:translation-constants-variables.title: Constants and Variables in a Translation
def:translation-constants-variables.statement_preview: A \emph{constant} names a fixed object from the translation key, while a \emph{variable} marks a place bound by a quantifier or left open for later binding.
def:translation-constants-variables.statement_display:
A \emph{constant} names a fixed object from the translation key, while a
\emph{variable} marks a place bound by a quantifier or left open for later
binding.
def:translation-constants-variables.statement_tex:
A \emph{constant} names a fixed object from the translation key, while a
\emph{variable} marks a place bound by a quantifier or left open for later
binding.

prop:universal-translation-pattern.title: Universal Translation Pattern
prop:universal-translation-pattern.statement_preview: In a broad domain, an English statement of the form ``All \(A\) are \(B\)'' is translated by \[ \forall x\,(A(x)\to B(x)). \]
prop:universal-translation-pattern.statement_display:
In a broad domain, an English statement of the form ``All \(A\) are \(B\)''
is translated by
\[
\forall x\,(A(x)\to B(x)).
\]
prop:universal-translation-pattern.statement_tex:
In a broad domain, an English statement of the form ``All \(A\) are \(B\)''
is translated by
\[
\forall x\,(A(x)\to B(x)).
\]

prop:existential-translation-pattern.title: Existential Translation Pattern
prop:existential-translation-pattern.statement_preview: In a broad domain, an English statement of the form ``Some \(A\) are \(B\)'' is translated by \[ \exists x\,(A(x)\land B(x)). \]
prop:existential-translation-pattern.statement_display:
In a broad domain, an English statement of the form ``Some \(A\) are \(B\)''
is translated by
\[
\exists x\,(A(x)\land B(x)).
\]
prop:existential-translation-pattern.statement_tex:
In a broad domain, an English statement of the form ``Some \(A\) are \(B\)''
is translated by
\[
\exists x\,(A(x)\land B(x)).
\]

prop:conditional-translation-pattern.title: Conditional Translation Pattern
prop:conditional-translation-pattern.statement_preview: An English statement of the form ``If \(A\), then \(B\)'' is translated by \[ A\to B, \] with quantifiers placed according to the variables occurring in \(A\) and \(B\).
prop:conditional-translation-pattern.statement_display:
An English statement of the form ``If \(A\), then \(B\)'' is translated by
\[
A\to B,
\]
with quantifiers placed according to the variables occurring in \(A\) and \(B\).
prop:conditional-translation-pattern.statement_tex:
An English statement of the form ``If \(A\), then \(B\)'' is translated by
\[
A\to B,
\]
with quantifiers placed according to the variables occurring in \(A\) and \(B\).

prop:negation-translation-pattern.title: Negation Translation Pattern
prop:negation-translation-pattern.statement_preview: The negation of a translated quantified statement is obtained by negating the whole formula and then pushing the negation inward using the quantifier negation laws.
prop:negation-translation-pattern.statement_display:
The negation of a translated quantified statement is obtained by negating the
whole formula and then pushing the negation inward using the quantifier
negation laws.
prop:negation-translation-pattern.statement_tex:
The negation of a translated quantified statement is obtained by negating the
whole formula and then pushing the negation inward using the quantifier
negation laws.

def:scope-ambiguity-translation.title: Scope Ambiguity
def:scope-ambiguity-translation.statement_preview: A sentence has \emph{scope ambiguity} when the same ordinary-language wording admits two or more translations with different quantifier nesting or different connective scope.
def:scope-ambiguity-translation.statement_display:
A sentence has \emph{scope ambiguity} when the same ordinary-language wording
admits two or more translations with different quantifier nesting or different
connective scope.
def:scope-ambiguity-translation.statement_tex:
A sentence has \emph{scope ambiguity} when the same ordinary-language wording
admits two or more translations with different quantifier nesting or different
connective scope.

def:categorical-a-form-predicate-logic.title: Universal Affirmative Form
def:categorical-a-form-predicate-logic.statement_preview: The \emph{universal affirmative} categorical form, called \(A\), translates ``All \(A\) are \(B\)'' as \[ \forall x\,(A(x)\to B(x)). \]
def:categorical-a-form-predicate-logic.statement_display:
The \emph{universal affirmative} categorical form, called \(A\), translates
``All \(A\) are \(B\)'' as
\[
\forall x\,(A(x)\to B(x)).
\]
def:categorical-a-form-predicate-logic.statement_tex:
The \emph{universal affirmative} categorical form, called \(A\), translates
``All \(A\) are \(B\)'' as
\[
\forall x\,(A(x)\to B(x)).
\]

def:categorical-e-form-predicate-logic.title: Universal Negative Form
def:categorical-e-form-predicate-logic.statement_preview: The \emph{universal negative} categorical form, called \(E\), translates ``No \(A\) are \(B\)'' as \[ \forall x\,(A(x)\to \neg B(x)). \]
def:categorical-e-form-predicate-logic.statement_display:
The \emph{universal negative} categorical form, called \(E\), translates
``No \(A\) are \(B\)'' as
\[
\forall x\,(A(x)\to \neg B(x)).
\]
def:categorical-e-form-predicate-logic.statement_tex:
The \emph{universal negative} categorical form, called \(E\), translates
``No \(A\) are \(B\)'' as
\[
\forall x\,(A(x)\to \neg B(x)).
\]

def:categorical-i-form-predicate-logic.title: Particular Affirmative Form
def:categorical-i-form-predicate-logic.statement_preview: The \emph{particular affirmative} categorical form, called \(I\), translates ``Some \(A\) are \(B\)'' as \[ \exists x\,(A(x)\land B(x)). \]
def:categorical-i-form-predicate-logic.statement_display:
The \emph{particular affirmative} categorical form, called \(I\), translates
``Some \(A\) are \(B\)'' as
\[
\exists x\,(A(x)\land B(x)).
\]
def:categorical-i-form-predicate-logic.statement_tex:
The \emph{particular affirmative} categorical form, called \(I\), translates
``Some \(A\) are \(B\)'' as
\[
\exists x\,(A(x)\land B(x)).
\]

def:categorical-o-form-predicate-logic.title: Particular Negative Form
def:categorical-o-form-predicate-logic.statement_preview: The \emph{particular negative} categorical form, called \(O\), translates ``Some \(A\) are not \(B\)'' as \[ \exists x\,(A(x)\land \neg B(x)). \]
def:categorical-o-form-predicate-logic.statement_display:
The \emph{particular negative} categorical form, called \(O\), translates
``Some \(A\) are not \(B\)'' as
\[
\exists x\,(A(x)\land \neg B(x)).
\]
def:categorical-o-form-predicate-logic.statement_tex:
The \emph{particular negative} categorical form, called \(O\), translates
``Some \(A\) are not \(B\)'' as
\[
\exists x\,(A(x)\land \neg B(x)).
\]

prop:a-o-contradictory-predicate-logic.title: A-O Contradiction
prop:a-o-contradictory-predicate-logic.statement_preview: The \(A\)-form \(\forall x\,(A(x)\to B(x))\) and the \(O\)-form \(\exists x\,(A(x)\land \neg B(x))\) are contradictory: exactly one of them is true.
prop:a-o-contradictory-predicate-logic.statement_display:
The \(A\)-form \(\forall x\,(A(x)\to B(x))\) and the \(O\)-form
\(\exists x\,(A(x)\land \neg B(x))\) are contradictory: exactly one of them is
true.
prop:a-o-contradictory-predicate-logic.statement_tex:
The \(A\)-form \(\forall x\,(A(x)\to B(x))\) and the \(O\)-form
\(\exists x\,(A(x)\land \neg B(x))\) are contradictory: exactly one of them is
true.

prop:e-i-contradictory-predicate-logic.title: E-I Contradiction
prop:e-i-contradictory-predicate-logic.statement_preview: The \(E\)-form \(\forall x\,(A(x)\to \neg B(x))\) and the \(I\)-form \(\exists x\,(A(x)\land B(x))\) are contradictory: exactly one of them is true.
prop:e-i-contradictory-predicate-logic.statement_display:
The \(E\)-form \(\forall x\,(A(x)\to \neg B(x))\) and the \(I\)-form
\(\exists x\,(A(x)\land B(x))\) are contradictory: exactly one of them is true.
prop:e-i-contradictory-predicate-logic.statement_tex:
The \(E\)-form \(\forall x\,(A(x)\to \neg B(x))\) and the \(I\)-form
\(\exists x\,(A(x)\land B(x))\) are contradictory: exactly one of them is true.

prop:existential-import-contrariety-pred.title: Contrariety Requires Existential Import
prop:existential-import-contrariety-pred.statement_preview: If \(\exists x\,A(x)\), then the \(A\)-form and \(E\)-form cannot both be true.
prop:existential-import-contrariety-pred.statement_display: If \(\exists x\,A(x)\), then the \(A\)-form and \(E\)-form cannot both be true.
prop:existential-import-contrariety-pred.statement_tex: If \(\exists x\,A(x)\), then the \(A\)-form and \(E\)-form cannot both be true.

def:propositional-equivalence-law.title: Propositional Equivalence Law
def:propositional-equivalence-law.statement_preview: A propositional equivalence law is a valid schema of the form \[ \varphi\equiv\psi, \] with \(\varphi,\psi\in\WFF\). Each instance of the schema says that the two formulas have the same truth value under every truth assignment.
def:propositional-equivalence-law.statement_display:
A propositional equivalence law is a valid schema of the form
\[
\varphi\equiv\psi,
\]
with \(\varphi,\psi\in\WFF\). Each instance of the schema says that the two formulas have the same truth value under every truth assignment.
def:propositional-equivalence-law.statement_tex:
A propositional equivalence law is a valid schema of the form
\[
\varphi\equiv\psi,
\]
with \(\varphi,\psi\in\WFF\). Each instance of the schema says that the two formulas have the same truth value under every truth assignment.

thm:logical-equivalence-equivalence-rela.title: Logical Equivalence is an Equivalence Relation
thm:logical-equivalence-equivalence-rela.statement_preview: Logical equivalence is reflexive, symmetric, and transitive on \(\WFF\): \[ \varphi\equiv\varphi, \qquad \varphi\equiv\psi\Longrightarrow\psi\equiv\varphi, \] and \[ (\varphi\equiv\psi\land\psi\equiv\chi) \Longrightarrow \varphi\equiv\chi. \]
thm:logical-equivalence-equivalence-rela.statement_display:
Logical equivalence is reflexive, symmetric, and transitive on \(\WFF\):
\[
\varphi\equiv\varphi,
\qquad
\varphi\equiv\psi\Longrightarrow\psi\equiv\varphi,
\]
and
\[
(\varphi\equiv\psi\land\psi\equiv\chi)
\Longrightarrow
\varphi\equiv\chi.
\]
thm:logical-equivalence-equivalence-rela.statement_tex:
Logical equivalence is reflexive, symmetric, and transitive on \(\WFF\):
\[
\varphi\equiv\varphi,
\qquad
\varphi\equiv\psi\Longrightarrow\psi\equiv\varphi,
\]
and
\[
(\varphi\equiv\psi\land\psi\equiv\chi)
\Longrightarrow
\varphi\equiv\chi.
\]

thm:basic-boolean-equivalence-laws-propo.title: Basic Boolean Equivalence Laws
thm:basic-boolean-equivalence-laws-propo.statement_preview: For all formulas \(\varphi,\psi,\chi\in\WFF\), the following logical equivalences hold. Commutativity: \[ \varphi\land\psi\equiv\psi\land\varphi, \qquad \varphi\lor\psi\equiv\psi\lor\varphi. \] Associativity: \[ (\varphi\land\psi)\land\chi \equiv \varphi\land(\psi\land\chi), \qquad (\varphi\lor\psi)\lor\chi \equiv \varphi\lor(\psi\lor\chi). \] Idempotence:...
thm:basic-boolean-equivalence-laws-propo.statement_display:
For all formulas \(\varphi,\psi,\chi\in\WFF\), the following logical equivalences hold.

Commutativity:
\[
\varphi\land\psi\equiv\psi\land\varphi,
\qquad
\varphi\lor\psi\equiv\psi\lor\varphi.
\]

Associativity:
\[
(\varphi\land\psi)\land\chi
\equiv
\varphi\land(\psi\land\chi),
\qquad
(\varphi\lor\psi)\lor\chi
\equiv
\varphi\lor(\psi\lor\chi).
\]

Idempotence:
\[
\varphi\land\varphi\equiv\varphi,
\qquad
\varphi\lor\varphi\equiv\varphi.
\]

Double negation:
\[
\neg\neg\varphi\equiv\varphi.
\]
thm:basic-boolean-equivalence-laws-propo.statement_tex:
For all formulas \(\varphi,\psi,\chi\in\WFF\), the following logical equivalences hold.

Commutativity:
\[
\varphi\land\psi\equiv\psi\land\varphi,
\qquad
\varphi\lor\psi\equiv\psi\lor\varphi.
\]

Associativity:
\[
(\varphi\land\psi)\land\chi
\equiv
\varphi\land(\psi\land\chi),
\qquad
(\varphi\lor\psi)\lor\chi
\equiv
\varphi\lor(\psi\lor\chi).
\]

Idempotence:
\[
\varphi\land\varphi\equiv\varphi,
\qquad
\varphi\lor\varphi\equiv\varphi.
\]

Double negation:
\[
\neg\neg\varphi\equiv\varphi.
\]

thm:identity-domination-laws-proposition.title: Identity and Domination Laws
thm:identity-domination-laws-proposition.statement_preview: Let \(\top\) be any tautological formula and let \(\bot\) be any contradictory formula. Then for every \(\varphi\in\WFF\): \[ \varphi\land\top\equiv\varphi, \qquad \varphi\lor\bot\equiv\varphi, \] and \[ \varphi\land\bot\equiv\bot, \qquad \varphi\lor\top\equiv\top. \]
thm:identity-domination-laws-proposition.statement_display:
Let \(\top\) be any tautological formula and let \(\bot\) be any contradictory formula. Then for every \(\varphi\in\WFF\):
\[
\varphi\land\top\equiv\varphi,
\qquad
\varphi\lor\bot\equiv\varphi,
\]
and
\[
\varphi\land\bot\equiv\bot,
\qquad
\varphi\lor\top\equiv\top.
\]
thm:identity-domination-laws-proposition.statement_tex:
Let \(\top\) be any tautological formula and let \(\bot\) be any contradictory formula. Then for every \(\varphi\in\WFF\):
\[
\varphi\land\top\equiv\varphi,
\qquad
\varphi\lor\bot\equiv\varphi,
\]
and
\[
\varphi\land\bot\equiv\bot,
\qquad
\varphi\lor\top\equiv\top.
\]

thm:de-morgan-laws-propositional-logic.title: De Morgan Laws for Propositional Logic

def:formula-context-propositional-logic.title: Formula Context
def:formula-context-propositional-logic.statement_preview: A formula context \(C[-]\) is a well-formed formula with one distinguished placeholder occurrence \([-]\). For each well-formed formula \(\varphi\), the expression \(C[\varphi]\) denotes the formula obtained by replacing the placeholder with \(\varphi\).
def:formula-context-propositional-logic.statement_display: A formula context \(C[-]\) is a well-formed formula with one distinguished placeholder occurrence \([-]\). For each well-formed formula \(\varphi\), the expression \(C[\varphi]\) denotes the formula obtained by replacing the placeholder with \(\varphi\).
def:formula-context-propositional-logic.statement_tex: A formula context \(C[-]\) is a well-formed formula with one distinguished placeholder occurrence \([-]\). For each well-formed formula \(\varphi\), the expression \(C[\varphi]\) denotes the formula obtained by replacing the placeholder with \(\varphi\).

thm:replacement-of-logical-equivalents-p.title: Replacement of Logical Equivalents
thm:replacement-of-logical-equivalents-p.statement_preview: Replacing logically equivalent formulas inside a formula context preserves logical equivalence: \[ \varphi\equiv\psi \Longrightarrow C[\varphi]\equiv C[\psi]. \]
thm:replacement-of-logical-equivalents-p.statement_display:
Replacing logically equivalent formulas inside a formula context preserves logical equivalence:
\[
\varphi\equiv\psi
\Longrightarrow
C[\varphi]\equiv C[\psi].
\]
thm:replacement-of-logical-equivalents-p.statement_tex:
Replacing logically equivalent formulas inside a formula context preserves logical equivalence:
\[
\varphi\equiv\psi
\Longrightarrow
C[\varphi]\equiv C[\psi].
\]

def:argument-form-propositional-logic.title: Argument Form
def:argument-form-propositional-logic.section_title: Argument Forms and Informal Proof Patterns
def:argument-form-propositional-logic.statement_preview: An \emph{argument form} is a schematic pattern consisting of a finite list of premise formulas and one conclusion formula.
def:argument-form-propositional-logic.statement_display:
An \emph{argument form} is a schematic pattern consisting of a finite list of
premise formulas and one conclusion formula.
def:argument-form-propositional-logic.statement_tex:
An \emph{argument form} is a schematic pattern consisting of a finite list of
premise formulas and one conclusion formula.

def:valid-argument-form-propositional-lo.title: Valid Argument Form
def:valid-argument-form-propositional-lo.section_title: Argument Forms and Informal Proof Patterns
def:valid-argument-form-propositional-lo.statement_preview: An argument form with premises \(\Gamma\) and conclusion \(\varphi\) is \emph{valid} if every truth assignment that satisfies every formula in \(\Gamma\) also satisfies \(\varphi\).
def:valid-argument-form-propositional-lo.statement_display:
An argument form with premises \(\Gamma\) and conclusion \(\varphi\) is
\emph{valid} if every truth assignment that satisfies every formula in
\(\Gamma\) also satisfies \(\varphi\).
def:valid-argument-form-propositional-lo.statement_tex:
An argument form with premises \(\Gamma\) and conclusion \(\varphi\) is
\emph{valid} if every truth assignment that satisfies every formula in
\(\Gamma\) also satisfies \(\varphi\).

def:counterexample-assignment-propositio.title: Counterexample Assignment
def:counterexample-assignment-propositio.section_title: Argument Forms and Informal Proof Patterns
def:counterexample-assignment-propositio.statement_preview: A \emph{counterexample assignment} to an argument form \((\Gamma,\varphi)\) is a truth assignment that satisfies every premise in \(\Gamma\) and does not satisfy \(\varphi\).
def:counterexample-assignment-propositio.statement_display:
A \emph{counterexample assignment} to an argument form \((\Gamma,\varphi)\) is a
truth assignment that satisfies every premise in \(\Gamma\) and does not satisfy
\(\varphi\).
def:counterexample-assignment-propositio.statement_tex:
A \emph{counterexample assignment} to an argument form \((\Gamma,\varphi)\) is a
truth assignment that satisfies every premise in \(\Gamma\) and does not satisfy
\(\varphi\).

thm:truth-table-validity-test-argument-f.title: Truth-Table Validity Test for Argument Forms
thm:truth-table-validity-test-argument-f.section_title: Argument Forms and Informal Proof Patterns
thm:truth-table-validity-test-argument-f.statement_preview: An argument form \((\Gamma,\varphi)\) is valid if and only if no row of its truth table makes every formula in \(\Gamma\) true and \(\varphi\) false.
thm:truth-table-validity-test-argument-f.statement_display:
An argument form \((\Gamma,\varphi)\) is valid if and only if no row of its truth
table makes every formula in \(\Gamma\) true and \(\varphi\) false.
thm:truth-table-validity-test-argument-f.statement_tex:
An argument form \((\Gamma,\varphi)\) is valid if and only if no row of its truth
table makes every formula in \(\Gamma\) true and \(\varphi\) false.

def:informal-proof-pattern-propositional.title: Informal Proof Pattern
def:informal-proof-pattern-propositional.section_title: Argument Forms and Informal Proof Patterns
def:informal-proof-pattern-propositional.statement_preview: An \emph{informal proof pattern} is a reusable truth-preserving strategy for organizing an ordinary mathematical proof.
def:informal-proof-pattern-propositional.statement_display:
An \emph{informal proof pattern} is a reusable truth-preserving strategy for
organizing an ordinary mathematical proof.
def:informal-proof-pattern-propositional.statement_tex:
An \emph{informal proof pattern} is a reusable truth-preserving strategy for
organizing an ordinary mathematical proof.

thm:affirming-consequent-invalid-proposi.title: Affirming the Consequent is Invalid
thm:affirming-consequent-invalid-proposi.section_title: Argument Forms and Informal Proof Patterns

thm:denying-antecedent-invalid-propositi.title: Denying the Antecedent is Invalid
thm:denying-antecedent-invalid-propositi.section_title: Argument Forms and Informal Proof Patterns

def:rule-of-replacement-propositional-lo.title: Rule of Replacement
def:rule-of-replacement-propositional-lo.section_title: Argument Forms and Informal Proof Patterns
def:rule-of-replacement-propositional-lo.statement_preview: A \emph{rule of replacement} is a schematic permission to replace a subformula by a logically equivalent formula inside a formula context.
def:rule-of-replacement-propositional-lo.statement_display:
A \emph{rule of replacement} is a schematic permission to replace a subformula
by a logically equivalent formula inside a formula context.
def:rule-of-replacement-propositional-lo.statement_tex:
A \emph{rule of replacement} is a schematic permission to replace a subformula
by a logically equivalent formula inside a formula context.

thm:replacement-rules-preserve-logical-e.title: Replacement Rules Preserve Logical Equivalence
thm:replacement-rules-preserve-logical-e.section_title: Argument Forms and Informal Proof Patterns
thm:replacement-rules-preserve-logical-e.statement_preview: If a formula \(\psi\) is obtained from a formula \(\varphi\) by finitely many applications of rules of replacement, then \(\varphi\equiv\psi\).
thm:replacement-rules-preserve-logical-e.statement_display:
If a formula \(\psi\) is obtained from a formula \(\varphi\) by finitely many
applications of rules of replacement, then \(\varphi\equiv\psi\).
thm:replacement-rules-preserve-logical-e.statement_tex:
If a formula \(\psi\) is obtained from a formula \(\varphi\) by finitely many
applications of rules of replacement, then \(\varphi\equiv\psi\).

thm:tautological-implication-valid-argum.title: Tautological Implication Gives a Valid Argument Form
thm:tautological-implication-valid-argum.section_title: Argument Forms and Informal Proof Patterns
thm:tautological-implication-valid-argum.statement_preview: Let \(\Gamma=\{\gamma_1,\ldots,\gamma_n\}\subseteq\WFF\) and let \(\varphi\in\WFF\). If \[ (\gamma_1\land\cdots\land\gamma_n)\to\varphi \] is a tautology, then the argument form \((\Gamma,\varphi)\) is valid.
thm:tautological-implication-valid-argum.statement_display:
Let \(\Gamma=\{\gamma_1,\ldots,\gamma_n\}\subseteq\WFF\) and let
\(\varphi\in\WFF\). If
\[
(\gamma_1\land\cdots\land\gamma_n)\to\varphi
\]
is a tautology, then the argument form \((\Gamma,\varphi)\) is valid.
thm:tautological-implication-valid-argum.statement_tex:
Let \(\Gamma=\{\gamma_1,\ldots,\gamma_n\}\subseteq\WFF\) and let
\(\varphi\in\WFF\). If
\[
(\gamma_1\land\cdots\land\gamma_n)\to\varphi
\]
is a tautology, then the argument form \((\Gamma,\varphi)\) is valid.

ax:propositional-weakening.title: Propositional Weakening
ax:propositional-weakening.statement_preview: For all formulas $P$ and $Q$, \[ P \Longrightarrow (Q \Longrightarrow P). \]
ax:propositional-weakening.statement_display:
For all formulas $P$ and $Q$,
\[
P \Longrightarrow (Q \Longrightarrow P).
\]
ax:propositional-weakening.statement_tex:
For all formulas $P$ and $Q$,
\[
P \Longrightarrow (Q \Longrightarrow P).
\]

ax:propositional-implication-distributio.title: Implication Distribution
ax:propositional-implication-distributio.statement_preview: For all formulas $P$, $Q$, and $R$, \[ \bigl(P \Longrightarrow (Q \Longrightarrow R)\bigr) \Longrightarrow \bigl((P \Longrightarrow Q) \Longrightarrow (P \Longrightarrow R)\bigr). \]
ax:propositional-implication-distributio.statement_display:
For all formulas $P$, $Q$, and $R$,
\[
\bigl(P \Longrightarrow (Q \Longrightarrow R)\bigr)
\Longrightarrow
\bigl((P \Longrightarrow Q) \Longrightarrow (P \Longrightarrow R)\bigr).
\]
ax:propositional-implication-distributio.statement_tex:
For all formulas $P$, $Q$, and $R$,
\[
\bigl(P \Longrightarrow (Q \Longrightarrow R)\bigr)
\Longrightarrow
\bigl((P \Longrightarrow Q) \Longrightarrow (P \Longrightarrow R)\bigr).
\]

ax:propositional-classical-contrapositio.title: Classical Contraposition
ax:propositional-classical-contrapositio.statement_preview: For all formulas $P$ and $Q$, \[ (\neg Q \Longrightarrow \neg P) \Longrightarrow (P \Longrightarrow Q). \]
ax:propositional-classical-contrapositio.statement_display:
For all formulas $P$ and $Q$,
\[
(\neg Q \Longrightarrow \neg P) \Longrightarrow (P \Longrightarrow Q).
\]
ax:propositional-classical-contrapositio.statement_tex:
For all formulas $P$ and $Q$,
\[
(\neg Q \Longrightarrow \neg P) \Longrightarrow (P \Longrightarrow Q).
\]

def:unsatisfiability-witness-proposition.title: Unsatisfiability Witness
def:unsatisfiability-witness-proposition.statement_preview: Let \(\Gamma\subseteq\WFF\). A subset \(\Delta\subseteq\Gamma\) is an unsatisfiability witness for \(\Gamma\) if \(\Delta\) is unsatisfiable.
def:unsatisfiability-witness-proposition.statement_display:
Let \(\Gamma\subseteq\WFF\). A subset \(\Delta\subseteq\Gamma\) is an
unsatisfiability witness for \(\Gamma\) if \(\Delta\) is unsatisfiable.
def:unsatisfiability-witness-proposition.statement_tex:
Let \(\Gamma\subseteq\WFF\). A subset \(\Delta\subseteq\Gamma\) is an
unsatisfiability witness for \(\Gamma\) if \(\Delta\) is unsatisfiable.

def:finite-unsatisfiability-witness-prop.title: Finite Unsatisfiability Witness
def:finite-unsatisfiability-witness-prop.statement_preview: Let \(\Gamma\subseteq\WFF\). A subset \(\Delta\subseteq\Gamma\) is a finite unsatisfiability witness for \(\Gamma\) if \(\Delta\) is finite and unsatisfiable. \[ \operatorname{FiniteUnsatisfiabilityWitness}(\Delta,\Gamma) \Longleftrightarrow \Delta\subseteq\Gamma\land \Delta\text{ is finite}\land \operatorname{UnsatisfiableSet}(\Delta). \]
def:finite-unsatisfiability-witness-prop.statement_display:
Let \(\Gamma\subseteq\WFF\). A subset \(\Delta\subseteq\Gamma\) is a finite
unsatisfiability witness for \(\Gamma\) if \(\Delta\) is finite and
unsatisfiable.
\[
\operatorname{FiniteUnsatisfiabilityWitness}(\Delta,\Gamma)
\Longleftrightarrow
\Delta\subseteq\Gamma\land \Delta\text{ is finite}\land
\operatorname{UnsatisfiableSet}(\Delta).
\]
def:finite-unsatisfiability-witness-prop.statement_tex:
Let \(\Gamma\subseteq\WFF\). A subset \(\Delta\subseteq\Gamma\) is a finite
unsatisfiability witness for \(\Gamma\) if \(\Delta\) is finite and
unsatisfiable.
\[
\operatorname{FiniteUnsatisfiabilityWitness}(\Delta,\Gamma)
\Longleftrightarrow
\Delta\subseteq\Gamma\land \Delta\text{ is finite}\land
\operatorname{UnsatisfiableSet}(\Delta).
\]

thm:finite-unsatisfiability-witness-prop.title: Finite Unsatisfiability Witness
thm:finite-unsatisfiability-witness-prop.statement_preview: Let \(\Gamma\subseteq\WFF\). If there exists a finite \(\Delta\subseteq\Gamma\) such that \(\Delta\) is unsatisfiable, then \(\Gamma\) is unsatisfiable.
thm:finite-unsatisfiability-witness-prop.statement_display:
Let \(\Gamma\subseteq\WFF\). If there exists a finite
\(\Delta\subseteq\Gamma\) such that \(\Delta\) is unsatisfiable, then
\(\Gamma\) is unsatisfiable.
thm:finite-unsatisfiability-witness-prop.statement_tex:
Let \(\Gamma\subseteq\WFF\). If there exists a finite
\(\Delta\subseteq\Gamma\) such that \(\Delta\) is unsatisfiable, then
\(\Gamma\) is unsatisfiable.

cor:finite-unsatisfiability-witness-cont.title: Finite Witness Contrapositive
cor:finite-unsatisfiability-witness-cont.statement_preview: Let \(\Gamma\subseteq\WFF\). If \(\Gamma\) is satisfiable, then every finite subset of \(\Gamma\) is satisfiable.
cor:finite-unsatisfiability-witness-cont.statement_display:
Let \(\Gamma\subseteq\WFF\). If \(\Gamma\) is satisfiable, then every finite
subset of \(\Gamma\) is satisfiable.
cor:finite-unsatisfiability-witness-cont.statement_tex:
Let \(\Gamma\subseteq\WFF\). If \(\Gamma\) is satisfiable, then every finite
subset of \(\Gamma\) is satisfiable.

def:finite-subset-of-set-of-propositiona.title: Finite Subset of a Set of Propositional Formulas
def:finite-subset-of-set-of-propositiona.statement_preview: Let \(\Gamma\subseteq\WFF\). A finite subset of \(\Gamma\) is a set \(\Delta\subseteq\Gamma\) containing only finitely many formulas.
def:finite-subset-of-set-of-propositiona.statement_display:
Let \(\Gamma\subseteq\WFF\). A finite subset of \(\Gamma\) is a set
\(\Delta\subseteq\Gamma\) containing only finitely many formulas.
def:finite-subset-of-set-of-propositiona.statement_tex:
Let \(\Gamma\subseteq\WFF\). A finite subset of \(\Gamma\) is a set
\(\Delta\subseteq\Gamma\) containing only finitely many formulas.

def:finitely-satisfiable-set-of-proposit.title: Finitely Satisfiable Set of Propositional Formulas
def:finitely-satisfiable-set-of-proposit.statement_preview: Let \(\Gamma\subseteq\WFF\). The set \(\Gamma\) is finitely satisfiable if every finite subset of \(\Gamma\) is satisfiable. \[ \operatorname{FinitelySatisfiableSet}(\Gamma) \Longleftrightarrow \forall\Delta\,\Bigl((\Delta\subseteq\Gamma\land \Delta\text{ is finite}) \Rightarrow \operatorname{SatisfiableSet}(\Delta)\Bigr). \]
def:finitely-satisfiable-set-of-proposit.statement_display:
Let \(\Gamma\subseteq\WFF\). The set \(\Gamma\) is finitely satisfiable if every
finite subset of \(\Gamma\) is satisfiable.
\[
\operatorname{FinitelySatisfiableSet}(\Gamma)
\Longleftrightarrow
\forall\Delta\,\Bigl((\Delta\subseteq\Gamma\land \Delta\text{ is finite})
\Rightarrow \operatorname{SatisfiableSet}(\Delta)\Bigr).
\]
def:finitely-satisfiable-set-of-proposit.statement_tex:
Let \(\Gamma\subseteq\WFF\). The set \(\Gamma\) is finitely satisfiable if every
finite subset of \(\Gamma\) is satisfiable.
\[
\operatorname{FinitelySatisfiableSet}(\Gamma)
\Longleftrightarrow
\forall\Delta\,\Bigl((\Delta\subseteq\Gamma\land \Delta\text{ is finite})
\Rightarrow \operatorname{SatisfiableSet}(\Delta)\Bigr).
\]

thm:propositional-compactness.title: Propositional Compactness
thm:propositional-compactness.statement_preview: Let \(\Gamma\subseteq\WFF\). If every finite subset of \(\Gamma\) is satisfiable, then \(\Gamma\) is satisfiable.
thm:propositional-compactness.statement_display:
Let \(\Gamma\subseteq\WFF\). If every finite subset of \(\Gamma\) is
satisfiable, then \(\Gamma\) is satisfiable.
thm:propositional-compactness.statement_tex:
Let \(\Gamma\subseteq\WFF\). If every finite subset of \(\Gamma\) is
satisfiable, then \(\Gamma\) is satisfiable.

def:satisfiable-set-of-propositional-for.title: Satisfiable Set of Propositional Formulas
def:satisfiable-set-of-propositional-for.statement_preview: Let \(\Gamma\subseteq\WFF\). The set \(\Gamma\) is satisfiable if there exists a truth assignment \(v\) such that \(v\models\Gamma\). \[ \operatorname{SatisfiableSet}(\Gamma) \Longleftrightarrow \exists v\,\bigl(\operatorname{TruthAssignment}(v)\land v\models\Gamma\bigr). \]
def:satisfiable-set-of-propositional-for.statement_display:
Let \(\Gamma\subseteq\WFF\). The set \(\Gamma\) is satisfiable if there exists a
truth assignment \(v\) such that \(v\models\Gamma\).
\[
\operatorname{SatisfiableSet}(\Gamma)
\Longleftrightarrow
\exists v\,\bigl(\operatorname{TruthAssignment}(v)\land v\models\Gamma\bigr).
\]
def:satisfiable-set-of-propositional-for.statement_tex:
Let \(\Gamma\subseteq\WFF\). The set \(\Gamma\) is satisfiable if there exists a
truth assignment \(v\) such that \(v\models\Gamma\).
\[
\operatorname{SatisfiableSet}(\Gamma)
\Longleftrightarrow
\exists v\,\bigl(\operatorname{TruthAssignment}(v)\land v\models\Gamma\bigr).
\]

def:unsatisfiable-set-of-propositional-f.title: Unsatisfiable Set of Propositional Formulas
def:unsatisfiable-set-of-propositional-f.statement_preview: Let \(\Gamma\subseteq\WFF\). The set \(\Gamma\) is unsatisfiable if it is not satisfiable. \[ \operatorname{UnsatisfiableSet}(\Gamma) \Longleftrightarrow \neg\operatorname{SatisfiableSet}(\Gamma). \]
def:unsatisfiable-set-of-propositional-f.statement_display:
Let \(\Gamma\subseteq\WFF\). The set \(\Gamma\) is unsatisfiable if it is not
satisfiable.
\[
\operatorname{UnsatisfiableSet}(\Gamma)
\Longleftrightarrow
\neg\operatorname{SatisfiableSet}(\Gamma).
\]
def:unsatisfiable-set-of-propositional-f.statement_tex:
Let \(\Gamma\subseteq\WFF\). The set \(\Gamma\) is unsatisfiable if it is not
satisfiable.
\[
\operatorname{UnsatisfiableSet}(\Gamma)
\Longleftrightarrow
\neg\operatorname{SatisfiableSet}(\Gamma).
\]

def:boolean-function-representation-prop.title: Representation of a Boolean Function
def:boolean-function-representation-prop.statement_preview: Let \(f:\mathbb{B}^n\to\mathbb{B}\) be a Boolean function. A formula \(\varphi\in\WFF\) with variables among \(P_1,\ldots,P_n\) \emph{represents} \(f\) if for every truth assignment \(v\), \[ \widehat v(\varphi)=f(v(P_1),\ldots,v(P_n)). \]
def:boolean-function-representation-prop.statement_display:
Let \(f:\mathbb{B}^n\to\mathbb{B}\) be a Boolean function. A formula
\(\varphi\in\WFF\) with variables among \(P_1,\ldots,P_n\) \emph{represents}
\(f\) if for every truth assignment \(v\),
\[
\widehat v(\varphi)=f(v(P_1),\ldots,v(P_n)).
\]
def:boolean-function-representation-prop.statement_tex:
Let \(f:\mathbb{B}^n\to\mathbb{B}\) be a Boolean function. A formula
\(\varphi\in\WFF\) with variables among \(P_1,\ldots,P_n\) \emph{represents}
\(f\) if for every truth assignment \(v\),
\[
\widehat v(\varphi)=f(v(P_1),\ldots,v(P_n)).
\]

thm:dnf-representation-of-boolean-functi.title: DNF Representation of Boolean Functions
thm:dnf-representation-of-boolean-functi.statement_preview: Every Boolean function \(f:\mathbb{B}^n\to\mathbb{B}\) is represented by a formula in disjunctive normal form using variables \(P_1,\ldots,P_n\).
thm:dnf-representation-of-boolean-functi.statement_display:
Every Boolean function \(f:\mathbb{B}^n\to\mathbb{B}\) is represented by a
formula in disjunctive normal form using variables \(P_1,\ldots,P_n\).
thm:dnf-representation-of-boolean-functi.statement_tex:
Every Boolean function \(f:\mathbb{B}^n\to\mathbb{B}\) is represented by a
formula in disjunctive normal form using variables \(P_1,\ldots,P_n\).

def:connective-basis-propositional-logic.title: Connective Basis
def:connective-basis-propositional-logic.statement_preview: A \emph{connective basis} is a specified set \(B\) of propositional connectives allowed as primitive connectives for building formulas.
def:connective-basis-propositional-logic.statement_display:
A \emph{connective basis} is a specified set \(B\) of propositional connectives
allowed as primitive connectives for building formulas.
def:connective-basis-propositional-logic.statement_tex:
A \emph{connective basis} is a specified set \(B\) of propositional connectives
allowed as primitive connectives for building formulas.

def:definable-connective-propositional-l.title: Definable Connective
def:definable-connective-propositional-l.statement_preview: Let \(B\) be a connective basis. A connective \(\circ\) is \emph{definable from} \(B\) if there is a formula built using only connectives from \(B\) whose truth function agrees with the truth function of \(\circ\).

def:functionally-complete-connective-set.title: Functionally Complete Connective Set
def:functionally-complete-connective-set.statement_preview: A connective basis \(B\) is \emph{functionally complete} if every Boolean function \(f:\mathbb{B}^n\to\mathbb{B}\), for every \(n\ge 1\), is represented by some formula using only connectives from \(B\).
def:functionally-complete-connective-set.statement_display:
A connective basis \(B\) is \emph{functionally complete} if every Boolean
function \(f:\mathbb{B}^n\to\mathbb{B}\), for every \(n\ge 1\), is represented
by some formula using only connectives from \(B\).
def:functionally-complete-connective-set.statement_tex:
A connective basis \(B\) is \emph{functionally complete} if every Boolean
function \(f:\mathbb{B}^n\to\mathbb{B}\), for every \(n\ge 1\), is represented
by some formula using only connectives from \(B\).

def:expressively-equivalent-connective-s.title: Expressively Equivalent Connective Sets
def:expressively-equivalent-connective-s.statement_preview: Two connective bases \(B\) and \(C\) are \emph{expressively equivalent} if every connective in \(B\) is definable from \(C\), and every connective in \(C\) is definable from \(B\).
def:expressively-equivalent-connective-s.statement_display:
Two connective bases \(B\) and \(C\) are \emph{expressively equivalent} if every
connective in \(B\) is definable from \(C\), and every connective in \(C\) is
definable from \(B\).
def:expressively-equivalent-connective-s.statement_tex:
Two connective bases \(B\) and \(C\) are \emph{expressively equivalent} if every
connective in \(B\) is definable from \(C\), and every connective in \(C\) is
definable from \(B\).

def:incomplete-connective-set-propositio.title: Incomplete Connective Set
def:incomplete-connective-set-propositio.statement_preview: A connective basis is \emph{incomplete} if it is not functionally complete.
def:incomplete-connective-set-propositio.statement_display: A connective basis is \emph{incomplete} if it is not functionally complete.
def:incomplete-connective-set-propositio.statement_tex: A connective basis is \emph{incomplete} if it is not functionally complete.

def:monotone-boolean-function-propositio.title: Monotone Boolean Function
def:monotone-boolean-function-propositio.statement_preview: A Boolean function \(f:\mathbb{B}^n\to\mathbb{B}\) is \emph{monotone} if changing input coordinates from \(\False\) to \(\True\) never changes the output from \(\True\) to \(\False\).
def:monotone-boolean-function-propositio.statement_display:
A Boolean function \(f:\mathbb{B}^n\to\mathbb{B}\) is \emph{monotone} if
changing input coordinates from \(\False\) to \(\True\) never changes the output
from \(\True\) to \(\False\).
def:monotone-boolean-function-propositio.statement_tex:
A Boolean function \(f:\mathbb{B}^n\to\mathbb{B}\) is \emph{monotone} if
changing input coordinates from \(\False\) to \(\True\) never changes the output
from \(\True\) to \(\False\).

thm:conjunction-disjunction-not-function.title: Conjunction and Disjunction are not Functionally Complete
thm:conjunction-disjunction-not-function.statement_preview: The connective basis \(\{\land,\lor\}\) is not functionally complete.
thm:conjunction-disjunction-not-function.statement_display: The connective basis \(\{\land,\lor\}\) is not functionally complete.
thm:conjunction-disjunction-not-function.statement_tex: The connective basis \(\{\land,\lor\}\) is not functionally complete.

def:minimal-complete-basis-propositional.title: Minimal Complete Basis
def:minimal-complete-basis-propositional.statement_preview: A functionally complete connective basis \(B\) is \emph{minimal complete} if no proper subset of \(B\) is functionally complete.
def:minimal-complete-basis-propositional.statement_display:
A functionally complete connective basis \(B\) is \emph{minimal complete} if no
proper subset of \(B\) is functionally complete.
def:minimal-complete-basis-propositional.statement_tex:
A functionally complete connective basis \(B\) is \emph{minimal complete} if no
proper subset of \(B\) is functionally complete.

thm:nand-nor-minimal-complete-bases-prop.title: NAND and NOR are Minimal Complete Bases
thm:nand-nor-minimal-complete-bases-prop.statement_preview: The one-connective bases \(\{\uparrow\}\) and \(\{\downarrow\}\) are minimal complete bases.
thm:nand-nor-minimal-complete-bases-prop.statement_display:
The one-connective bases \(\{\uparrow\}\) and \(\{\downarrow\}\) are minimal
complete bases.
thm:nand-nor-minimal-complete-bases-prop.statement_tex:
The one-connective bases \(\{\uparrow\}\) and \(\{\downarrow\}\) are minimal
complete bases.

def:nand-connective-propositional-logic.title: NAND Connective
def:nand-connective-propositional-logic.statement_preview: The \emph{NAND} connective, written \(\uparrow\), is the binary connective defined by \[ \varphi\uparrow\psi\equiv\neg(\varphi\land\psi). \]
def:nand-connective-propositional-logic.statement_display:
The \emph{NAND} connective, written \(\uparrow\), is the binary connective
defined by
\[
\varphi\uparrow\psi\equiv\neg(\varphi\land\psi).
\]
def:nand-connective-propositional-logic.statement_tex:
The \emph{NAND} connective, written \(\uparrow\), is the binary connective
defined by
\[
\varphi\uparrow\psi\equiv\neg(\varphi\land\psi).
\]

def:nand-only-formula-propositional-logi.title: NAND-Only Formula
def:nand-only-formula-propositional-logi.statement_preview: A \emph{NAND-only formula} is a well-formed formula whose only connective is \(\uparrow\).
def:nand-only-formula-propositional-logi.statement_display:
A \emph{NAND-only formula} is a well-formed formula whose only connective is
\(\uparrow\).
def:nand-only-formula-propositional-logi.statement_tex:
A \emph{NAND-only formula} is a well-formed formula whose only connective is
\(\uparrow\).

thm:nand-functionally-complete-propositi.title: NAND is Functionally Complete
thm:nand-functionally-complete-propositi.statement_preview: The one-connective basis \(\{\uparrow\}\) is functionally complete.
thm:nand-functionally-complete-propositi.statement_display: The one-connective basis \(\{\uparrow\}\) is functionally complete.
thm:nand-functionally-complete-propositi.statement_tex: The one-connective basis \(\{\uparrow\}\) is functionally complete.

def:nor-connective-propositional-logic.title: NOR Connective
def:nor-connective-propositional-logic.statement_preview: The \emph{NOR} connective, written \(\downarrow\), is the binary connective defined by \[ \varphi\downarrow\psi\equiv\neg(\varphi\lor\psi). \]
def:nor-connective-propositional-logic.statement_display:
The \emph{NOR} connective, written \(\downarrow\), is the binary connective
defined by
\[
\varphi\downarrow\psi\equiv\neg(\varphi\lor\psi).
\]
def:nor-connective-propositional-logic.statement_tex:
The \emph{NOR} connective, written \(\downarrow\), is the binary connective
defined by
\[
\varphi\downarrow\psi\equiv\neg(\varphi\lor\psi).
\]

def:nor-only-formula-propositional-logic.title: NOR-Only Formula
def:nor-only-formula-propositional-logic.statement_preview: A \emph{NOR-only formula} is a well-formed formula whose only connective is \(\downarrow\).
def:nor-only-formula-propositional-logic.statement_display:
A \emph{NOR-only formula} is a well-formed formula whose only connective is
\(\downarrow\).
def:nor-only-formula-propositional-logic.statement_tex:
A \emph{NOR-only formula} is a well-formed formula whose only connective is
\(\downarrow\).

thm:nor-functionally-complete-propositio.title: NOR is Functionally Complete
thm:nor-functionally-complete-propositio.statement_preview: The one-connective basis \(\{\downarrow\}\) is functionally complete.
thm:nor-functionally-complete-propositio.statement_display: The one-connective basis \(\{\downarrow\}\) is functionally complete.
thm:nor-functionally-complete-propositio.statement_tex: The one-connective basis \(\{\downarrow\}\) is functionally complete.

def:connective-reduction-propositional-l.title: Connective Reduction
def:connective-reduction-propositional-l.statement_preview: A \emph{connective reduction} replaces a formula using one connective basis by a logically equivalent formula using a smaller or different connective basis.
def:connective-reduction-propositional-l.statement_display:
A \emph{connective reduction} replaces a formula using one connective basis by
a logically equivalent formula using a smaller or different connective basis.
def:connective-reduction-propositional-l.statement_tex:
A \emph{connective reduction} replaces a formula using one connective basis by
a logically equivalent formula using a smaller or different connective basis.

thm:standard-connectives-functionally-co.title: Standard Connectives are Functionally Complete
thm:standard-connectives-functionally-co.statement_preview: The connective basis \[ \{\neg,\land,\lor\} \] is functionally complete.

\[
\{\neg,\land,\lor\}
\]
is functionally complete.

def:truth-table-row-conjunction-proposit.title: Truth-Table Row Conjunction
def:truth-table-row-conjunction-proposit.statement_preview: Fix a finite variable list \(P_1,\ldots,P_n\) and a row \(r\) of the \hyperref[def:truth-table-propositional-logic]{truth table} for those variables. The \emph{truth-table row conjunction} for \(r\) is the conjunction \[ L_1\land L_2\land\cdots\land L_n, \] where \[ L_i= \begin{cases} P_i, & \text{if row }r\text{ makes }P_i\text{ true},\\ \neg P_i, & \text{...
def:truth-table-row-conjunction-proposit.statement_display:
Fix a finite variable list \(P_1,\ldots,P_n\) and a row \(r\) of the
\hyperref[def:truth-table-propositional-logic]{truth table} for those
variables. The \emph{truth-table row conjunction} for \(r\) is the conjunction
\[
L_1\land L_2\land\cdots\land L_n,
\]
where
\[
L_i=
\begin{cases}
P_i, & \text{if row }r\text{ makes }P_i\text{ true},\\
\neg P_i, & \text{if row }r\text{ makes }P_i\text{ false}.
\end{cases}
\]
def:truth-table-row-conjunction-proposit.statement_tex:
Fix a finite variable list \(P_1,\ldots,P_n\) and a row \(r\) of the
\hyperref[def:truth-table-propositional-logic]{truth table} for those
variables. The \emph{truth-table row conjunction} for \(r\) is the conjunction
\[
L_1\land L_2\land\cdots\land L_n,
\]
where
\[
L_i=
\begin{cases}
P_i, & \text{if row }r\text{ makes }P_i\text{ true},\\
\neg P_i, & \text{if row }r\text{ makes }P_i\text{ false}.
\end{cases}
\]

def:canonical-disjunctive-normal-form-pr.title: Canonical Disjunctive Normal Form
def:canonical-disjunctive-normal-form-pr.statement_preview: Let \(\varphi\in\WFF\), and fix an ordering \(P_1,\ldots,P_n\) of the finite variable set of \(\varphi\). The \emph{canonical disjunctive normal form} of \(\varphi\) relative to this ordering is the disjunction of the \hyperref[def:truth-table-row-conjunction-propositional-logic]{truth-table row conjunctions} corresponding to the rows on which \(\varphi\) i...
def:canonical-disjunctive-normal-form-pr.statement_display:
Let \(\varphi\in\WFF\), and fix an ordering \(P_1,\ldots,P_n\) of the finite
variable set of \(\varphi\). The \emph{canonical disjunctive normal form} of
\(\varphi\) relative to this ordering is the disjunction of the
\hyperref[def:truth-table-row-conjunction-propositional-logic]{truth-table row conjunctions}
corresponding to the rows on which \(\varphi\) is true.
def:canonical-disjunctive-normal-form-pr.statement_tex:

\hyperref[def:truth-table-row-conjunction-propositional-logic]{truth-table row conjunctions}

def:canonical-conjunctive-normal-form-pr.title: Canonical Conjunctive Normal Form
def:canonical-conjunctive-normal-form-pr.statement_preview: Let \(\varphi\in\WFF\), and fix an ordering \(P_1,\ldots,P_n\) of the finite variable set of \(\varphi\). The \emph{canonical conjunctive normal form} of \(\varphi\) relative to this ordering is the conjunction of the \hyperref[def:truth-table-row-clause-propositional-logic]{truth-table row clauses} corresponding to the rows on which \(\varphi\) is false.

thm:canonical-disjunctive-normal-form-pr.title: Canonical Disjunctive Normal Form Theorem
thm:canonical-disjunctive-normal-form-pr.statement_preview: Every formula \(\varphi\in\WFF\) is \hyperref[def:logical-equivalence-propositional-logic]{logically equivalent} to its \hyperref[def:canonical-disjunctive-normal-form-propositional-logic]{canonical disjunctive normal form} relative to a fixed ordering of its finitely many variables. \[ \varphi\equiv \operatorname{CDNF}_{P_1,\ldots,P_n}(\varphi). \]

to its
\hyperref[def:canonical-disjunctive-normal-form-propositional-logic]{canonical disjunctive normal form}
relative to a fixed ordering of its finitely many variables.
\[
\varphi\equiv \operatorname{CDNF}_{P_1,\ldots,P_n}(\varphi).
\]

def:elementary-disjunction-propositional.title: Elementary Disjunction
def:elementary-disjunction-propositional.statement_preview: An \emph{elementary disjunction} is a finite disjunction of \hyperref[def:literal-propositional-logic]{literals}. That is, it is a \hyperref[def:clause-propositional-logic]{clause} of the form \[ L_1\lor L_2\lor\cdots\lor L_k, \] where each \(L_i\) is a literal. The one-literal case is permitted.
def:elementary-disjunction-propositional.statement_display:
An \emph{elementary disjunction} is a finite disjunction of
\hyperref[def:literal-propositional-logic]{literals}. That is, it is a
\hyperref[def:clause-propositional-logic]{clause} of the form
\[
L_1\lor L_2\lor\cdots\lor L_k,
\]
where each \(L_i\) is a literal. The one-literal case is permitted.
def:elementary-disjunction-propositional.statement_tex:
An \emph{elementary disjunction} is a finite disjunction of
\hyperref[def:literal-propositional-logic]{literals}. That is, it is a

\[

\]

def:maxterm-propositional-logic.title: Maxterm
def:maxterm-propositional-logic.statement_preview: Let \(P_1,\ldots,P_n\) be a finite list of propositional variables. A \emph{maxterm} relative to \(P_1,\ldots,P_n\) is an \hyperref[def:elementary-disjunction-propositional-logic]{elementary disjunction} that contains exactly one of \(P_i\) or \(\neg P_i\) for each \(i=1,\ldots,n\).
def:maxterm-propositional-logic.statement_display:
Let \(P_1,\ldots,P_n\) be a finite list of propositional variables. A
\emph{maxterm} relative to \(P_1,\ldots,P_n\) is an
\hyperref[def:elementary-disjunction-propositional-logic]{elementary disjunction}
that contains exactly one of \(P_i\) or \(\neg P_i\) for each
\(i=1,\ldots,n\).
def:maxterm-propositional-logic.statement_tex:

def:conjunctive-normal-form-propositiona.title: Conjunctive Normal Form
def:conjunctive-normal-form-propositiona.statement_preview: A formula is in \emph{conjunctive normal form}, abbreviated \emph{CNF}, when it is a finite conjunction of \hyperref[def:clause-propositional-logic]{clauses}. Equivalently, it has the shape \[ C_1\land C_2\land\cdots\land C_m, \] where each \(C_j\) is a clause.
def:conjunctive-normal-form-propositiona.statement_display:
A formula is in \emph{conjunctive normal form}, abbreviated \emph{CNF}, when it
is a finite conjunction of

shape
\[
C_1\land C_2\land\cdots\land C_m,
\]
where each \(C_j\) is a clause.

thm:existence-conjunctive-normal-form-pr.title: Existence of Conjunctive Normal Form
thm:existence-conjunctive-normal-form-pr.statement_preview: For every formula \(\varphi\in\WFF\), there exists a formula \(\psi\in\WFF\) such that \(\psi\) is in \hyperref[def:conjunctive-normal-form-propositional-logic]{conjunctive normal form} and \[ \varphi\equiv\psi. \]

\(\psi\in\WFF\) such that \(\psi\) is in

and
\[
\varphi\equiv\psi.
\]

def:elementary-conjunction-propositional.title: Elementary Conjunction

That is, it is a \hyperref[def:term-propositional-logic]{term} of the form \[ L_1\land L_2\land\cdots\land L_k, \] where each \(L_i\) is a literal. The one-literal case is permitted.

def:minterm-propositional-logic.title: Minterm

A \emph{minterm} relative to \(P_1,\ldots,P_n\) is an \hyperref[def:elementary-conjunction-propositional-logic]{elementary conjunction} that contains exactly one of \(P_i\) or \(\neg P_i\) for each \(i=1,\ldots,n\).

def:disjunctive-normal-form-propositiona.title: Disjunctive Normal Form
def:disjunctive-normal-form-propositiona.statement_preview: A formula is in \emph{disjunctive normal form}, abbreviated \emph{DNF}, when it is a finite disjunction of \hyperref[def:elementary-conjunction-propositional-logic]{elementary conjunctions}. Equivalently, it has the shape \[ C_1\lor C_2\lor\cdots\lor C_m, \] where each \(C_j\) is an elementary conjunction.

thm:dnf-satisfiability-criterion-proposi.title: DNF Satisfiability Criterion
thm:dnf-satisfiability-criterion-proposi.statement_preview: Let \(\psi\) be a formula in \hyperref[def:disjunctive-normal-form-propositional-logic]{disjunctive normal form}. Then \(\psi\) is \hyperref[def:satisfiable-formula-propositional-logic]{satisfiable} if and only if at least one conjunction term in \(\psi\) contains no \hyperref[def:complementary-literals-propositional-logic]{complementary pair of literals}.
thm:dnf-satisfiability-criterion-proposi.statement_display:
Let \(\psi\) be a formula in

if at least one conjunction term in \(\psi\) contains no
\hyperref[def:complementary-literals-propositional-logic]{complementary pair of literals}.
