cor:complete-preorder-extension.statement_preview: For any nonempty set $X$ and preorder $\succsim$ on $X$, there exists a complete preorder that is an extension of $\succsim$.
cor:complete-preorder-extension.statement_display:
For any nonempty set $X$ and preorder $\succsim$ on $X$, there exists a
complete preorder that is an extension of $\succsim$.
cor:complete-preorder-extension.statement_tex:
For any nonempty set $X$ and preorder $\succsim$ on $X$, there exists a
complete preorder that is an extension of $\succsim$.

def:owc.title: and Proposition (Only Weak Cycles)
def:owc.statement_preview: Let $\succsim$ be a binary relation on $X$ with asymmetric part $\succ$ and transitive closure $T(\succsim)$. We say $\succsim$ satisfies \emph{only weak cycles} (OWC) if \[ xT(\succsim)y \;\Rightarrow\; \lnot(y \succ x). \] \textbf{Proposition.} A binary relation $\succsim$ on a nonempty set $X$ can be extended to a complete preorder if and only if it sati...
def:owc.statement_display:
Let $\succsim$ be a binary relation on $X$ with asymmetric part $\succ$ and
transitive closure $T(\succsim)$. We say $\succsim$ satisfies \emph{only weak
cycles} (OWC) if
\[
  xT(\succsim)y \;\Rightarrow\; \lnot(y \succ x).
\]

\textbf{Proposition.} A binary relation $\succsim$ on a nonempty
set $X$ can be extended to a complete preorder if and only if it satisfies
OWC.
def:owc.statement_tex:
Let $\succsim$ be a binary relation on $X$ with asymmetric part $\succ$ and
transitive closure $T(\succsim)$. We say $\succsim$ satisfies \emph{only weak
cycles} (OWC) if
\[
  xT(\succsim)y \;\Rightarrow\; \lnot(y \succ x).
\]

\textbf{Proposition.} A binary relation $\succsim$ on a nonempty
set $X$ can be extended to a complete preorder if and only if it satisfies
OWC.

def:cover-relation.title: Cover Relation
def:cover-relation.statement_preview: Let $(A, \leq)$ be a poset and $a, b \in A$. We say $b$ \emph{covers} $a$, written $a \lessdot b$, if $a < b$ and there is no $c \in A$ with $a < c < b$.
def:cover-relation.statement_display:
Let $(A, \leq)$ be a poset and $a, b \in A$.  We say $b$ \emph{covers} $a$,
written $a \lessdot b$, if $a < b$ and there is no $c \in A$ with
$a < c < b$.
def:cover-relation.statement_tex:
Let $(A, \leq)$ be a poset and $a, b \in A$.  We say $b$ \emph{covers} $a$,
written $a \lessdot b$, if $a < b$ and there is no $c \in A$ with
$a < c < b$.

def:hasse.title: Hasse Diagram
def:hasse.statement_preview: The \emph{Hasse diagram} of a finite poset $(A, \leq)$ is the directed graph whose vertices are the elements of $A$, with an upward edge from $a$ to $b$ whenever $a \lessdot b$. Three graphical conventions apply: \begin{enumerate}[label=(\roman*)] \item Reflexive loops ($a \leq a$) are suppressed. \item Edges implied by transitivity are suppressed: if $a \l...
def:hasse.statement_display:
The \emph{Hasse diagram} of a finite poset $(A, \leq)$ is the directed graph
whose vertices are the elements of $A$, with an upward edge from $a$ to $b$
whenever $a \lessdot b$.  Three graphical conventions apply:
\begin{enumerate}[label=(\roman*)]
  \item Reflexive loops ($a \leq a$) are suppressed.
  \item Edges implied by transitivity are suppressed: if $a \lessdot b$ and
        $b \lessdot c$, no direct edge from $a$ to $c$ is drawn.
  \item Direction is encoded by height: $a \lessdot b$ is drawn with $b$
        strictly above $a$, so arrowheads are unnecessary.
\end{enumerate}
def:hasse.statement_tex:
The \emph{Hasse diagram} of a finite poset $(A, \leq)$ is the directed graph
whose vertices are the elements of $A$, with an upward edge from $a$ to $b$
whenever $a \lessdot b$.  Three graphical conventions apply:
\begin{enumerate}[label=(\roman*)]
  \item Reflexive loops ($a \leq a$) are suppressed.
  \item Edges implied by transitivity are suppressed: if $a \lessdot b$ and
        $b \lessdot c$, no direct edge from $a$ to $c$ is drawn.
  \item Direction is encoded by height: $a \lessdot b$ is drawn with $b$
        strictly above $a$, so arrowheads are unnecessary.
\end{enumerate}

def:dual-poset.title: Dual Poset
def:dual-poset.statement_preview: Let $(A, \leq)$ be a poset. The \emph{dual poset} is $(A, \geq)$, where $a \geq b$ is defined to mean $b \leq a$. The dual is also written $(A, \leq^{\mathrm{op}})$.
def:dual-poset.statement_display:
Let $(A, \leq)$ be a poset.  The \emph{dual poset} is $(A, \geq)$, where
$a \geq b$ is defined to mean $b \leq a$.  The dual is also written
$(A, \leq^{\mathrm{op}})$.
def:dual-poset.statement_tex:
Let $(A, \leq)$ be a poset.  The \emph{dual poset} is $(A, \geq)$, where
$a \geq b$ is defined to mean $b \leq a$.  The dual is also written
$(A, \leq^{\mathrm{op}})$.

prop:order-duality.title: Order Duality
prop:order-duality.statement_preview: Let $\Phi$ be any first-order statement about a poset $(A, \leq)$ expressed using only the relation $\leq$. Let $\Phi^*$ be the statement obtained from $\Phi$ by replacing every occurrence of $\leq$ with $\geq$ (equivalently, working in the dual poset). Then: \[ (A, \leq) \models \Phi \;\;\Longleftrightarrow\;\; (A, \geq) \models \Phi. \] In particular, if...
prop:order-duality.statement_display:
Let $\Phi$ be any first-order statement about a poset $(A, \leq)$ expressed
using only the relation $\leq$.  Let $\Phi^*$ be the statement obtained from
$\Phi$ by replacing every occurrence of $\leq$ with $\geq$ (equivalently,
working in the dual poset).  Then:
\[
  (A, \leq) \models \Phi
  \;\;\Longleftrightarrow\;\;
  (A, \geq) \models \Phi.
\]
In particular, if $\Phi$ is a theorem about all posets, then so is $\Phi^*$.
prop:order-duality.statement_tex:
Let $\Phi$ be any first-order statement about a poset $(A, \leq)$ expressed
using only the relation $\leq$.  Let $\Phi^*$ be the statement obtained from
$\Phi$ by replacing every occurrence of $\leq$ with $\geq$ (equivalently,
working in the dual poset).  Then:
\[
  (A, \leq) \models \Phi
  \;\;\Longleftrightarrow\;\;
  (A, \geq) \models \Phi.
\]
In particular, if $\Phi$ is a theorem about all posets, then so is $\Phi^*$.

def:induced-order.title: Induced Order
def:induced-order.statement_preview: Let $(B, \leq')$ be a partially ordered set and let $f : A \to B$ be a function. The \emph{order induced by $f$} (or \emph{pullback order along $f$}) is the relation $\leq_f$ on $A$ defined by: \[ x \;\leq_f\; y \;\;\Longleftrightarrow\;\; f(x) \;\leq'\; f(y). \]
def:induced-order.statement_display:
Let $(B, \leq')$ be a partially ordered set and let $f : A \to B$ be a
function. The \emph{order induced by $f$} (or \emph{pullback order along
$f$}) is the relation $\leq_f$ on $A$ defined by:
\[
x \;\leq_f\; y
\;\;\Longleftrightarrow\;\;
f(x) \;\leq'\; f(y).
\]
def:induced-order.statement_tex:
Let $(B, \leq')$ be a partially ordered set and let $f : A \to B$ be a
function. The \emph{order induced by $f$} (or \emph{pullback order along
$f$}) is the relation $\leq_f$ on $A$ defined by:
\[
x \;\leq_f\; y
\;\;\Longleftrightarrow\;\;
f(x) \;\leq'\; f(y).
\]

prop:induced-preorder.title: Induced Preorder
prop:induced-preorder.statement_preview: For any function $f : A \to B$ and partial order $(B, \leq')$, the relation $\leq_f$ is a preorder on $A$: it is reflexive and transitive.
prop:induced-preorder.statement_display:
For any function $f : A \to B$ and partial order $(B, \leq')$, the relation
$\leq_f$ is a preorder on $A$: it is reflexive and transitive.
prop:induced-preorder.statement_tex:
For any function $f : A \to B$ and partial order $(B, \leq')$, the relation
$\leq_f$ is a preorder on $A$: it is reflexive and transitive.

def:f-indist.title: $f$-Indistinguishable Elements
def:f-indist.statement_preview: Let $f : A \to B$ and $\leq_f$ be the induced order. Two elements $x, y \in A$ are \emph{$f$-indistinguishable}, written $x \sim_f y$, if both $x \leq_f y$ and $y \leq_f x$, i.e.\ if \[ f(x) \leq' f(y) \quad \text{and} \quad f(y) \leq' f(x). \]
def:f-indist.statement_display:
Let $f : A \to B$ and $\leq_f$ be the induced order. Two elements
$x, y \in A$ are \emph{$f$-indistinguishable}, written $x \sim_f y$,
if both $x \leq_f y$ and $y \leq_f x$, i.e.\ if
\[
f(x) \leq' f(y) \quad \text{and} \quad f(y) \leq' f(x).
\]
def:f-indist.statement_tex:
Let $f : A \to B$ and $\leq_f$ be the induced order. Two elements
$x, y \in A$ are \emph{$f$-indistinguishable}, written $x \sim_f y$,
if both $x \leq_f y$ and $y \leq_f x$, i.e.\ if
\[
f(x) \leq' f(y) \quad \text{and} \quad f(y) \leq' f(x).
\]

prop:induced-poset.title: Induced Poset
prop:induced-poset.statement_preview: Let $(B, \leq')$ be a partially ordered set and $f : A \to B$. The induced order $\leq_f$ is a partial order on $A$ if and only if $f$ is injective.
prop:induced-poset.statement_display:
Let $(B, \leq')$ be a partially ordered set and $f : A \to B$.
The induced order $\leq_f$ is a partial order on $A$ if and only if
$f$ is injective.
prop:induced-poset.statement_tex:
Let $(B, \leq')$ be a partially ordered set and $f : A \to B$.
The induced order $\leq_f$ is a partial order on $A$ if and only if
$f$ is injective.

def:suborder.title: Suborder / Restriction
def:suborder.statement_preview: Let $(B, \leq')$ be a partially ordered set and $S \subseteq B$. The \emph{suborder} on $S$ (or \emph{induced order on $S$}) is the relation $\leq'_S$ defined by: \[ x \;\leq'_S\; y \;\;\Longleftrightarrow\;\; x \leq' y, \quad \text{for } x, y \in S. \]
def:suborder.statement_display:
Let $(B, \leq')$ be a partially ordered set and $S \subseteq B$. The
\emph{suborder} on $S$ (or \emph{induced order on $S$}) is the relation
$\leq'_S$ defined by:
\[
x \;\leq'_S\; y
\;\;\Longleftrightarrow\;\;
x \leq' y,
\quad \text{for } x, y \in S.
\]
def:suborder.statement_tex:
Let $(B, \leq')$ be a partially ordered set and $S \subseteq B$. The
\emph{suborder} on $S$ (or \emph{induced order on $S$}) is the relation
$\leq'_S$ defined by:
\[
x \;\leq'_S\; y
\;\;\Longleftrightarrow\;\;
x \leq' y,
\quad \text{for } x, y \in S.
\]

def:order-embedding.title: Order Embedding
def:order-embedding.statement_preview: Let $(A, \leq)$ and $(B, \leq')$ be partially ordered sets. A function $f : A \to B$ is an \emph{order embedding} if for all $x, y \in A$: \[ x \;\leq\; y \;\;\Longleftrightarrow\;\; f(x) \;\leq'\; f(y). \]
def:order-embedding.statement_display:
Let $(A, \leq)$ and $(B, \leq')$ be partially ordered sets. A function
$f : A \to B$ is an \emph{order embedding} if for all $x, y \in A$:
\[
x \;\leq\; y
\;\;\Longleftrightarrow\;\;
f(x) \;\leq'\; f(y).
\]
def:order-embedding.statement_tex:
Let $(A, \leq)$ and $(B, \leq')$ be partially ordered sets. A function
$f : A \to B$ is an \emph{order embedding} if for all $x, y \in A$:
\[
x \;\leq\; y
\;\;\Longleftrightarrow\;\;
f(x) \;\leq'\; f(y).
\]

prop:embedding-iso.title: Order embedding is isomorphism onto image
prop:embedding-iso.statement_preview: If $f : (A, \leq) \to (B, \leq')$ is an order embedding, then $f$ is an order isomorphism from $(A, \leq)$ to the suborder $(f(A), \leq'_{f(A)})$.
prop:embedding-iso.statement_display:
If $f : (A, \leq) \to (B, \leq')$ is an order embedding, then $f$ is an
order isomorphism from $(A, \leq)$ to the suborder $(f(A), \leq'_{f(A)})$.
prop:embedding-iso.statement_tex:
If $f : (A, \leq) \to (B, \leq')$ is an order embedding, then $f$ is an
order isomorphism from $(A, \leq)$ to the suborder $(f(A), \leq'_{f(A)})$.

def:lattice.title: Lattice
def:lattice.statement_preview: A \emph{lattice} is a poset $(L, \leq)$ in which every two-element subset $\{a, b\} \subseteq L$ has both a supremum and an infimum in $L$. The supremum $\sup\{a,b\}$ is called the \emph{join} of $a$ and $b$, written $a \vee b$. The infimum $\inf\{a,b\}$ is called the \emph{meet} of $a$ and $b$, written $a \wedge b$.
def:lattice.statement_display:
A \emph{lattice} is a poset $(L, \leq)$ in which every two-element subset
$\{a, b\} \subseteq L$ has both a supremum and an infimum in $L$.

The supremum $\sup\{a,b\}$ is called the \emph{join} of $a$ and $b$,
written $a \vee b$.
The infimum $\inf\{a,b\}$ is called the \emph{meet} of $a$ and $b$,
written $a \wedge b$.
def:lattice.statement_tex:
A \emph{lattice} is a poset $(L, \leq)$ in which every two-element subset
$\{a, b\} \subseteq L$ has both a supremum and an infimum in $L$.

The supremum $\sup\{a,b\}$ is called the \emph{join} of $a$ and $b$,
written $a \vee b$.
The infimum $\inf\{a,b\}$ is called the \emph{meet} of $a$ and $b$,
written $a \wedge b$.

def:complete-lattice.title: Complete Lattice
def:complete-lattice.statement_preview: A lattice $(L, \leq)$ is a \emph{complete lattice} if every subset $S \subseteq L$ (including the empty set and $L$ itself) has both a supremum and an infimum in $L$: \[ \forall S \subseteq L,\quad \sup S \in L \;\text{ and }\; \inf S \in L. \]
def:complete-lattice.statement_display:
A lattice $(L, \leq)$ is a \emph{complete lattice} if every subset
$S \subseteq L$ (including the empty set and $L$ itself) has both a
supremum and an infimum in $L$:
\[
  \forall S \subseteq L,\quad \sup S \in L \;\text{ and }\; \inf S \in L.
\]
def:complete-lattice.statement_tex:
A lattice $(L, \leq)$ is a \emph{complete lattice} if every subset
$S \subseteq L$ (including the empty set and $L$ itself) has both a
supremum and an infimum in $L$:
\[
  \forall S \subseteq L,\quad \sup S \in L \;\text{ and }\; \inf S \in L.
\]

def:distributive-lattice.title: Distributive Lattice
def:distributive-lattice.statement_preview: A lattice $(L, \leq)$ is \emph{distributive} if for all $a, b, c \in L$: \[ a \wedge (b \vee c) = (a \wedge b) \vee (a \wedge c). \]
def:distributive-lattice.statement_display:
A lattice $(L, \leq)$ is \emph{distributive} if for all $a, b, c \in L$:
\[
  a \wedge (b \vee c) = (a \wedge b) \vee (a \wedge c).
\]
def:distributive-lattice.statement_tex:
A lattice $(L, \leq)$ is \emph{distributive} if for all $a, b, c \in L$:
\[
  a \wedge (b \vee c) = (a \wedge b) \vee (a \wedge c).
\]

def:boolean-lattice.title: Complement and Boolean Lattice
def:boolean-lattice.statement_preview: Let $(L, \leq)$ be a bounded distributive lattice with top element $\top$ and bottom element $\bot$. An element $a' \in L$ is a \emph{complement} of $a \in L$ if \[ a \vee a' = \top \quad \text{and} \quad a \wedge a' = \bot. \] A \emph{Boolean lattice} (or \emph{Boolean algebra}) is a bounded distributive lattice in which every element has a complement.
def:boolean-lattice.statement_display:
Let $(L, \leq)$ be a bounded distributive lattice with top element $\top$ and
bottom element $\bot$.  An element $a' \in L$ is a \emph{complement} of
$a \in L$ if
\[
  a \vee a' = \top \quad \text{and} \quad a \wedge a' = \bot.
\]
A \emph{Boolean lattice} (or \emph{Boolean algebra}) is a bounded distributive
lattice in which every element has a complement.
def:boolean-lattice.statement_tex:
Let $(L, \leq)$ be a bounded distributive lattice with top element $\top$ and
bottom element $\bot$.  An element $a' \in L$ is a \emph{complement} of
$a \in L$ if
\[
  a \vee a' = \top \quad \text{and} \quad a \wedge a' = \bot.
\]
A \emph{Boolean lattice} (or \emph{Boolean algebra}) is a bounded distributive
lattice in which every element has a complement.

def:sup-inf.title: Supremum and Infimum
def:sup-inf.statement_preview: Let $(A, \leq)$ be a poset and $S \subseteq A$. The \emph{supremum} (or \emph{least upper bound}) of $S$, written $\sup S$, is an element $u^* \in A$ satisfying: \begin{enumerate}[label=(\roman*)] \item $u^*$ is an upper bound of $S$: $\forall s \in S,\ s \leq u^*$; \item $u^*$ is the \emph{least} upper bound: if $u$ is \emph{any} upper bound of $S$, then $...

prop:sup-unique.title: Sup Unique
prop:sup-unique.statement_preview: Let $(A, \leq)$ be a poset and $S \subseteq A$. If $\sup S$ exists, it is unique. If $\inf S$ exists, it is unique.
prop:sup-unique.statement_display:
Let $(A, \leq)$ be a poset and $S \subseteq A$.  If $\sup S$ exists, it is
unique.  If $\inf S$ exists, it is unique.
prop:sup-unique.statement_tex:
Let $(A, \leq)$ be a poset and $S \subseteq A$.  If $\sup S$ exists, it is
unique.  If $\inf S$ exists, it is unique.

prop:sup-char.title: Sup Char
prop:sup-char.statement_preview: Let $(A, \leq)$ be a poset, $S \subseteq A$, and $u^* \in A$. Then $u^* = \sup S$ if and only if: \begin{enumerate}[label=(\roman*)] \item $u^*$ is an upper bound of $S$; and \item for every $v \in A$ with $v < u^*$, there exists $s \in S$ with $v < s$. \end{enumerate}
prop:sup-char.statement_display:
Let $(A, \leq)$ be a poset, $S \subseteq A$, and $u^* \in A$.  Then
$u^* = \sup S$ if and only if:
\begin{enumerate}[label=(\roman*)]
  \item $u^*$ is an upper bound of $S$; and
  \item for every $v \in A$ with $v < u^*$, there exists $s \in S$ with
        $v < s$.
\end{enumerate}
prop:sup-char.statement_tex:
Let $(A, \leq)$ be a poset, $S \subseteq A$, and $u^* \in A$.  Then
$u^* = \sup S$ if and only if:
\begin{enumerate}[label=(\roman*)]
  \item $u^*$ is an upper bound of $S$; and
  \item for every $v \in A$ with $v < u^*$, there exists $s \in S$ with
        $v < s$.
\end{enumerate}

prop:sup-inf-duality.title: Sup Inf Duality
prop:sup-inf-duality.statement_preview: Let $(A, \leq)$ be a poset and $S \subseteq A$. Then $\inf_{(A,\leq)} S = \sup_{(A,\geq)} S$, where $(A,\geq)$ denotes the dual poset. In particular: if every nonempty bounded-above subset of $(A,\leq)$ has a supremum, then every nonempty bounded-below subset of $(A,\leq)$ has an infimum.
prop:sup-inf-duality.statement_display:
Let $(A, \leq)$ be a poset and $S \subseteq A$.  Then
$\inf_{(A,\leq)} S = \sup_{(A,\geq)} S$,
where $(A,\geq)$ denotes the dual poset.

In particular: if every nonempty bounded-above subset of $(A,\leq)$ has a
supremum, then every nonempty bounded-below subset of $(A,\leq)$ has an
infimum.
prop:sup-inf-duality.statement_tex:
Let $(A, \leq)$ be a poset and $S \subseteq A$.  Then
$\inf_{(A,\leq)} S = \sup_{(A,\geq)} S$,
where $(A,\geq)$ denotes the dual poset.

In particular: if every nonempty bounded-above subset of $(A,\leq)$ has a
supremum, then every nonempty bounded-below subset of $(A,\leq)$ has an
infimum.

def:ordered-set.title: Ordered Set
def:ordered-set.statement_preview: An \emph{ordered set} (or \emph{partially ordered set}, \emph{poset}) is a pair $(A,\leq)$ where $\leq$ is a partial order on $A$: a relation that is reflexive, antisymmetric, and transitive.
def:ordered-set.statement_display:
An \emph{ordered set} (or \emph{partially ordered set}, \emph{poset}) is a
pair $(A,\leq)$ where $\leq$ is a partial order on $A$: a relation that is
reflexive, antisymmetric, and transitive.
def:ordered-set.statement_tex:
An \emph{ordered set} (or \emph{partially ordered set}, \emph{poset}) is a
pair $(A,\leq)$ where $\leq$ is a partial order on $A$: a relation that is
reflexive, antisymmetric, and transitive.

def:strict-order.title: Strict Order
def:strict-order.statement_preview: Let $(A,\leq)$ be an ordered set. The \emph{strict order} $<$ is defined by: \[ a < b \;\;\Longleftrightarrow\;\; (a \leq b \land a \neq b). \]
def:strict-order.statement_display:
Let $(A,\leq)$ be an ordered set. The \emph{strict order} $<$ is defined by:
\[
a < b \;\;\Longleftrightarrow\;\; (a \leq b \land a \neq b).
\]
def:strict-order.statement_tex:
Let $(A,\leq)$ be an ordered set. The \emph{strict order} $<$ is defined by:
\[
a < b \;\;\Longleftrightarrow\;\; (a \leq b \land a \neq b).
\]

def:comparable.title: Comparable and Incomparable Elements
def:comparable.statement_preview: In an ordered set $(A,\leq)$, elements $a,b \in A$ are \emph{comparable} if $a \leq b$ or $b \leq a$; they are \emph{incomparable} if neither holds.
def:comparable.statement_display:
In an ordered set $(A,\leq)$, elements $a,b \in A$ are \emph{comparable} if
$a \leq b$ or $b \leq a$; they are \emph{incomparable} if neither holds.
def:comparable.statement_tex:
In an ordered set $(A,\leq)$, elements $a,b \in A$ are \emph{comparable} if
$a \leq b$ or $b \leq a$; they are \emph{incomparable} if neither holds.

def:linear-order.title: Total / Linear Order
def:linear-order.statement_preview: An ordered set $(A,\leq)$ is a \emph{total order} (or \emph{linear order}) if every pair of elements is comparable: \[ \forall a,b \in A,\; a \leq b \lor b \leq a. \]
def:linear-order.statement_display:
An ordered set $(A,\leq)$ is a \emph{total order} (or \emph{linear order})
if every pair of elements is comparable:
\[
\forall a,b \in A,\; a \leq b \lor b \leq a.
\]
def:linear-order.statement_tex:
An ordered set $(A,\leq)$ is a \emph{total order} (or \emph{linear order})
if every pair of elements is comparable:
\[
\forall a,b \in A,\; a \leq b \lor b \leq a.
\]

def:bounds.title: Upper and Lower Bounds
def:bounds.statement_preview: Let $(A,\leq)$ be an ordered set and $S \subseteq A$. An element $u \in A$ is an \emph{upper bound} of $S$ if $\forall s \in S,\; s \leq u$. An element $\ell \in A$ is a \emph{lower bound} of $S$ if $\forall s \in S,\; \ell \leq s$.
def:bounds.statement_display:
Let $(A,\leq)$ be an ordered set and $S \subseteq A$.

An element $u \in A$ is an \emph{upper bound} of $S$ if $\forall s \in S,\; s \leq u$.

An element $\ell \in A$ is a \emph{lower bound} of $S$ if $\forall s \in S,\; \ell \leq s$.
def:bounds.statement_tex:
Let $(A,\leq)$ be an ordered set and $S \subseteq A$.

An element $u \in A$ is an \emph{upper bound} of $S$ if $\forall s \in S,\; s \leq u$.

An element $\ell \in A$ is a \emph{lower bound} of $S$ if $\forall s \in S,\; \ell \leq s$.

def:well-order.title: Well-Ordered Set
def:well-order.statement_preview: An ordered set $(A,<)$ is \emph{well-ordered} if every nonempty subset $S \subseteq A$ has a least element: \[ \forall S \subseteq A,\; \bigl(S \neq \varnothing \;\Rightarrow\; \exists m \in S \text{ s.t.\ } m \leq s \;\forall s \in S\bigr). \]
def:well-order.statement_display:
An ordered set $(A,<)$ is \emph{well-ordered} if every nonempty subset
$S \subseteq A$ has a least element:
\[
\forall S \subseteq A,\;
\bigl(S \neq \varnothing \;\Rightarrow\; \exists m \in S \text{ s.t.\ }
m \leq s \;\forall s \in S\bigr).
\]
def:well-order.statement_tex:
An ordered set $(A,<)$ is \emph{well-ordered} if every nonempty subset
$S \subseteq A$ has a least element:
\[
\forall S \subseteq A,\;
\bigl(S \neq \varnothing \;\Rightarrow\; \exists m \in S \text{ s.t.\ }
m \leq s \;\forall s \in S\bigr).
\]

def:chain.title: Chain and Antichain
def:chain.statement_preview: A subset $C \subseteq A$ of a poset $(A,\leq)$ is a \emph{chain} if every pair of elements in $C$ is comparable. It is an \emph{antichain} if no two distinct elements of $C$ are comparable.
def:chain.statement_display:
A subset $C \subseteq A$ of a poset $(A,\leq)$ is a \emph{chain} if every
pair of elements in $C$ is comparable. It is an \emph{antichain} if no two
distinct elements of $C$ are comparable.
def:chain.statement_tex:
A subset $C \subseteq A$ of a poset $(A,\leq)$ is a \emph{chain} if every
pair of elements in $C$ is comparable. It is an \emph{antichain} if no two
distinct elements of $C$ are comparable.

def:initial-segment.title: Initial Segment
def:initial-segment.statement_preview: A subset $I \subseteq A$ is an \emph{initial segment} of $(A,\leq)$ if \[ a \in I \text{ and } b \leq a \;\Rightarrow\; b \in I. \]
def:initial-segment.statement_display:
A subset $I \subseteq A$ is an \emph{initial segment} of $(A,\leq)$ if
\[
a \in I \text{ and } b \leq a \;\Rightarrow\; b \in I.
\]
def:initial-segment.statement_tex:
A subset $I \subseteq A$ is an \emph{initial segment} of $(A,\leq)$ if
\[
a \in I \text{ and } b \leq a \;\Rightarrow\; b \in I.
\]

def:directed-set.title: Directed Set
def:directed-set.statement_preview: A preorder $(I, \leq)$ is \emph{directed} (or a \emph{directed set}) if every finite subset of $I$ has an upper bound in $I$: for all $i, j \in I$ there exists $k \in I$ with $i \leq k$ and $j \leq k$.
def:directed-set.statement_display:
A preorder $(I, \leq)$ is \emph{directed} (or a \emph{directed set}) if every
finite subset of $I$ has an upper bound in $I$: for all $i, j \in I$ there
exists $k \in I$ with $i \leq k$ and $j \leq k$.
def:directed-set.statement_tex:
A preorder $(I, \leq)$ is \emph{directed} (or a \emph{directed set}) if every
finite subset of $I$ has an upper bound in $I$: for all $i, j \in I$ there
exists $k \in I$ with $i \leq k$ and $j \leq k$.

def:net.title: Net
def:net.statement_preview: Let $(I, \leq)$ be a directed set and $X$ a set. A \emph{net} in $X$ is a function $x : I \to X$. The value at $i \in I$ is written $x_i$, and the net is written $(x_i)_{i \in I}$.
def:net.statement_display:
Let $(I, \leq)$ be a directed set and $X$ a set. A \emph{net} in $X$ is a
function $x : I \to X$. The value at $i \in I$ is written $x_i$, and the
net is written $(x_i)_{i \in I}$.
def:net.statement_tex:
Let $(I, \leq)$ be a directed set and $X$ a set. A \emph{net} in $X$ is a
function $x : I \to X$. The value at $i \in I$ is written $x_i$, and the
net is written $(x_i)_{i \in I}$.

def:reflexive.title: Reflexive Relation
def:reflexive.statement_preview: A binary relation $R\subseteq A\times A$ is \emph{reflexive} on $A$ if every element of the domain is related to itself: \[ \forall a\in A,\ (a,a)\in R. \] In infix notation, this is the condition $\forall a\in A,\ aRa$.
def:reflexive.statement_display:
A binary relation $R\subseteq A\times A$ is \emph{reflexive} on $A$ if every
element of the domain is related to itself:
\[
  \forall a\in A,\ (a,a)\in R.
\]
In infix notation, this is the condition $\forall a\in A,\ aRa$.
def:reflexive.statement_tex:
A binary relation $R\subseteq A\times A$ is \emph{reflexive} on $A$ if every
element of the domain is related to itself:
\[
  \forall a\in A,\ (a,a)\in R.
\]
In infix notation, this is the condition $\forall a\in A,\ aRa$.

def:irreflexive.title: Irreflexive Relation
def:irreflexive.statement_preview: A binary relation $R\subseteq A\times A$ is \emph{irreflexive} on $A$ if no element of the domain is related to itself: \[ \forall a\in A,\ (a,a)\notin R. \] In infix notation, this is the condition $\forall a\in A,\ \neg(aRa)$.
def:irreflexive.statement_display:
A binary relation $R\subseteq A\times A$ is \emph{irreflexive} on $A$ if no
element of the domain is related to itself:
\[
  \forall a\in A,\ (a,a)\notin R.
\]
In infix notation, this is the condition $\forall a\in A,\ \neg(aRa)$.
def:irreflexive.statement_tex:
A binary relation $R\subseteq A\times A$ is \emph{irreflexive} on $A$ if no
element of the domain is related to itself:
\[
  \forall a\in A,\ (a,a)\notin R.
\]
In infix notation, this is the condition $\forall a\in A,\ \neg(aRa)$.

def:symmetric.title: Symmetric Relation
def:symmetric.statement_preview: A binary relation $R\subseteq A\times A$ is \emph{symmetric} on $A$ if every related pair also appears in the reverse order: \[ \forall a,b\in A,\ (a,b)\in R \rightarrow (b,a)\in R. \] In infix notation, this is $\forall a,b\in A,\ aRb\rightarrow bRa$.
def:symmetric.statement_display:
A binary relation $R\subseteq A\times A$ is \emph{symmetric} on $A$ if every
related pair also appears in the reverse order:
\[
  \forall a,b\in A,\ (a,b)\in R \rightarrow (b,a)\in R.
\]
In infix notation, this is $\forall a,b\in A,\ aRb\rightarrow bRa$.
def:symmetric.statement_tex:
A binary relation $R\subseteq A\times A$ is \emph{symmetric} on $A$ if every
related pair also appears in the reverse order:
\[
  \forall a,b\in A,\ (a,b)\in R \rightarrow (b,a)\in R.
\]
In infix notation, this is $\forall a,b\in A,\ aRb\rightarrow bRa$.

def:antisymmetric.title: Antisymmetric Relation
def:antisymmetric.statement_preview: A binary relation $R\subseteq A\times A$ is \emph{antisymmetric} on $A$ if any two elements related in both directions must be equal: \[ \forall a,b\in A,\; \bigl((a,b)\in R\land(b,a)\in R\bigr)\rightarrow a=b. \] In infix notation, this is $\forall a,b\in A,\ ((aRb\land bRa)\rightarrow a=b)$.
def:antisymmetric.statement_display:
A binary relation $R\subseteq A\times A$ is \emph{antisymmetric} on $A$ if any
two elements related in both directions must be equal:
\[
  \forall a,b\in A,\;
  \bigl((a,b)\in R\land(b,a)\in R\bigr)\rightarrow a=b.
\]
In infix notation, this is
$\forall a,b\in A,\ ((aRb\land bRa)\rightarrow a=b)$.
def:antisymmetric.statement_tex:
A binary relation $R\subseteq A\times A$ is \emph{antisymmetric} on $A$ if any
two elements related in both directions must be equal:
\[
  \forall a,b\in A,\;
  \bigl((a,b)\in R\land(b,a)\in R\bigr)\rightarrow a=b.
\]
In infix notation, this is
$\forall a,b\in A,\ ((aRb\land bRa)\rightarrow a=b)$.

def:asymmetric.title: Asymmetric Relation
def:asymmetric.statement_preview: A binary relation $R\subseteq A\times A$ is \emph{asymmetric} on $A$ if every related pair excludes the reverse pair: \[ \forall a,b\in A,\; (a,b)\in R \rightarrow (b,a)\notin R. \] In infix notation, this is $\forall a,b\in A,\ (aRb\rightarrow\neg(bRa))$.
def:asymmetric.statement_display:
A binary relation $R\subseteq A\times A$ is \emph{asymmetric} on $A$ if every
related pair excludes the reverse pair:
\[
  \forall a,b\in A,\;
  (a,b)\in R \rightarrow (b,a)\notin R.
\]
In infix notation, this is
$\forall a,b\in A,\ (aRb\rightarrow\neg(bRa))$.
def:asymmetric.statement_tex:
A binary relation $R\subseteq A\times A$ is \emph{asymmetric} on $A$ if every
related pair excludes the reverse pair:
\[
  \forall a,b\in A,\;
  (a,b)\in R \rightarrow (b,a)\notin R.
\]
In infix notation, this is
$\forall a,b\in A,\ (aRb\rightarrow\neg(bRa))$.

def:transitive.title: Transitive Relation
def:transitive.statement_preview: A binary relation $R\subseteq A\times A$ is \emph{transitive} on $A$ if any two composable related pairs collapse to a direct related pair: \[ \forall a,b,c\in A,\; \bigl((a,b)\in R\land(b,c)\in R\bigr)\rightarrow(a,c)\in R. \] In infix notation, this is $\forall a,b,c\in A,\ ((aRb\land bRc)\rightarrow aRc)$.
def:transitive.statement_display:
A binary relation $R\subseteq A\times A$ is \emph{transitive} on $A$ if any
two composable related pairs collapse to a direct related pair:
\[
  \forall a,b,c\in A,\;
  \bigl((a,b)\in R\land(b,c)\in R\bigr)\rightarrow(a,c)\in R.
\]
In infix notation, this is
$\forall a,b,c\in A,\ ((aRb\land bRc)\rightarrow aRc)$.
def:transitive.statement_tex:
A binary relation $R\subseteq A\times A$ is \emph{transitive} on $A$ if any
two composable related pairs collapse to a direct related pair:
\[
  \forall a,b,c\in A,\;
  \bigl((a,b)\in R\land(b,c)\in R\bigr)\rightarrow(a,c)\in R.
\]
In infix notation, this is
$\forall a,b,c\in A,\ ((aRb\land bRc)\rightarrow aRc)$.

def:total-rel.title: Total (Connex) Relation
def:total-rel.statement_preview: $R$ is \emph{total} (or \emph{connex}) if every pair is comparable: \[ \forall a,b \in A, \; (a,b) \in R \lor (b,a) \in R. \]
def:total-rel.statement_display:
$R$ is \emph{total} (or \emph{connex}) if every pair is comparable:
\[
\forall a,b \in A, \;
(a,b) \in R \lor (b,a) \in R.
\]
def:total-rel.statement_tex:
$R$ is \emph{total} (or \emph{connex}) if every pair is comparable:
\[
\forall a,b \in A, \;
(a,b) \in R \lor (b,a) \in R.
\]

def:equivalence-rel.title: Equivalence Relation
def:equivalence-rel.statement_preview: $R$ is an \emph{equivalence relation} on $A$ if it is reflexive, symmetric, and transitive.
def:equivalence-rel.statement_display:
$R$ is an \emph{equivalence relation} on $A$ if it is reflexive, symmetric,
and transitive.
def:equivalence-rel.statement_tex:
$R$ is an \emph{equivalence relation} on $A$ if it is reflexive, symmetric,
and transitive.

def:preorder.title: Preorder
def:preorder.statement_preview: $R$ is a \emph{preorder} on $A$ if it is reflexive and transitive.
def:preorder.statement_display: $R$ is a \emph{preorder} on $A$ if it is reflexive and transitive.
def:preorder.statement_tex: $R$ is a \emph{preorder} on $A$ if it is reflexive and transitive.

def:partial-order.title: Partial Order
def:partial-order.statement_preview: $R$ is a \emph{partial order} on $A$ if it is reflexive, antisymmetric, and transitive.
def:partial-order.statement_display:
$R$ is a \emph{partial order} on $A$ if it is reflexive, antisymmetric, and
transitive.
def:partial-order.statement_tex:
$R$ is a \emph{partial order} on $A$ if it is reflexive, antisymmetric, and
transitive.

def:total-order.title: Total Order
def:total-order.statement_preview: $R$ is a \emph{total order} on $A$ if it is a partial order and is total.
def:total-order.statement_display: $R$ is a \emph{total order} on $A$ if it is a partial order and is total.
def:total-order.statement_tex: $R$ is a \emph{total order} on $A$ if it is a partial order and is total.

def:ordered-pair.title: Ordered Pair
def:ordered-pair.statement_preview: Let $a$ and $b$ be sets. The \emph{ordered pair} $(a,b)$ is defined (Kuratowski) as \[ (a,b) \;:=\; \{\{a\}, \{a,b\}\}. \]
def:ordered-pair.statement_display:
Let $a$ and $b$ be sets. The \emph{ordered pair} $(a,b)$ is defined (Kuratowski)
as
\[
(a,b) \;:=\; \{\{a\}, \{a,b\}\}.
\]
def:ordered-pair.statement_tex:
Let $a$ and $b$ be sets. The \emph{ordered pair} $(a,b)$ is defined (Kuratowski)
as
\[
(a,b) \;:=\; \{\{a\}, \{a,b\}\}.
\]

thm:ordered-pair-unique.title: Uniqueness of Ordered Pairs
thm:ordered-pair-unique.statement_preview: For any sets $a,b,c,d$, \[ (a,b) = (c,d) \;\;\Longleftrightarrow\;\; (a = c \land b = d). \]
thm:ordered-pair-unique.statement_display:
For any sets $a,b,c,d$,
\[
(a,b) = (c,d)
\;\;\Longleftrightarrow\;\;
(a = c \land b = d).
\]
thm:ordered-pair-unique.statement_tex:
For any sets $a,b,c,d$,
\[
(a,b) = (c,d)
\;\;\Longleftrightarrow\;\;
(a = c \land b = d).
\]

def:cartesian-product-rel.title: Cartesian Product — as foundation for relations
def:cartesian-product-rel.statement_preview: The \emph{Cartesian product} of sets $A$ and $B$ is: \[ A \times B \;:=\; \{\, (a,b) \mid a \in A \text{ and } b \in B \,\}. \]
def:cartesian-product-rel.statement_display:
The \emph{Cartesian product} of sets $A$ and $B$ is:
\[
A \times B
\;:=\;
\{\, (a,b) \mid a \in A \text{ and } b \in B \,\}.
\]
def:cartesian-product-rel.statement_tex:
The \emph{Cartesian product} of sets $A$ and $B$ is:
\[
A \times B
\;:=\;
\{\, (a,b) \mid a \in A \text{ and } b \in B \,\}.
\]

def:relation.title: Relation
def:relation.statement_preview: A \emph{relation} from $A$ to $B$ is any subset $R \subseteq A \times B$. If $(a,b) \in R$ we write $a \, R \, b$ and say ``$a$ is related to $b$.'' When $A = B$ we say $R$ is a \emph{relation on $A$}.
def:relation.statement_display:
A \emph{relation} from $A$ to $B$ is any subset $R \subseteq A \times B$.

If $(a,b) \in R$ we write $a \, R \, b$ and say ``$a$ is related to $b$.''
When $A = B$ we say $R$ is a \emph{relation on $A$}.
def:relation.statement_tex:
A \emph{relation} from $A$ to $B$ is any subset $R \subseteq A \times B$.

If $(a,b) \in R$ we write $a \, R \, b$ and say ``$a$ is related to $b$.''
When $A = B$ we say $R$ is a \emph{relation on $A$}.

def:semiring-of-sets.title: Semiring of Sets
def:semiring-of-sets.statement_preview: A collection $\mathcal{S}$ of subsets of a set $X$ is a \emph{semiring} if: \begin{enumerate}[label=(\roman*)] \item $\varnothing \in \mathcal{S}$; \item $A, B \in \mathcal{S}$ implies $A \cap B \in \mathcal{S}$; \item for any $A, B \in \mathcal{S}$, the difference $A \setminus B$ is a \emph{finite disjoint union} of members of $\mathcal{S}$: there exist pa...

def:ring-of-setsxxx.title: Ring of Sets
def:ring-of-setsxxx.statement_preview: A collection $\mathcal{R}$ of subsets of $X$ is a \emph{ring of sets} if: \begin{enumerate}[label=(\roman*)] \item $\varnothing \in \mathcal{R}$; \item $A, B \in \mathcal{R}$ implies $A \cup B \in \mathcal{R}$; \item $A, B \in \mathcal{R}$ implies $A \setminus B \in \mathcal{R}$. \end{enumerate}

def:algebra-of-sets.title: Algebra of Sets
def:algebra-of-sets.statement_preview: A collection $\mathcal{A}$ of subsets of $X$ is an \emph{algebra of sets} (or \emph{Boolean algebra of sets}, or \emph{field of sets}) if: \begin{enumerate}[label=(\roman*)] \item $X \in \mathcal{A}$; \item $A \in \mathcal{A}$ implies $A^c = X \setminus A \in \mathcal{A}$; \item $A, B \in \mathcal{A}$ implies $A \cup B \in \mathcal{A}$. \end{enumerate}

def:sigma-algebra.title: $\sigma$-Algebra
def:sigma-algebra.statement_preview: A collection $\mathcal{M}$ of subsets of $X$ is a \emph{$\sigma$-algebra} (or \emph{$\sigma$-field}) on $X$ if: \begin{enumerate}[label=(\roman*)] \item $X \in \mathcal{M}$; \item $A \in \mathcal{M}$ implies $A^c \in \mathcal{M}$; \item if $\{A_n\}_{n \in \mathbb{N}}$ is a countable family in $\mathcal{M}$, then $\bigcup_{n=1}^{\infty} A_n \in \mathcal{M}$....

def:topology-defn.title: Topology
def:topology-defn.statement_preview: A \emph{topology} on a set $X$ is a collection $\tau$ of subsets of $X$ satisfying: \begin{enumerate}[label=(\roman*)] \item $\varnothing \in \tau$ and $X \in \tau$; \item if $\{U_\alpha\}_{\alpha \in I}$ is any family of sets in $\tau$ (indexed by any set $I$), then $\bigcup_{\alpha \in I} U_\alpha \in \tau$; \item if $U_1, \dots, U_n \in \tau$ (finitely m...
def:topology-defn.statement_display:
A \emph{topology} on a set $X$ is a collection $\tau$ of subsets of $X$
satisfying:
\begin{enumerate}[label=(\roman*)]
\item $\varnothing \in \tau$ and $X \in \tau$;
\item if $\{U_\alpha\}_{\alpha \in I}$ is any family of sets in $\tau$ (indexed
  by any set $I$), then $\bigcup_{\alpha \in I} U_\alpha \in \tau$;
\item if $U_1, \dots, U_n \in \tau$ (finitely many), then $U_1 \cap \cdots
  \cap U_n \in \tau$.
\end{enumerate}
The pair $(X, \tau)$ is a \emph{topological space}. Members of $\tau$ are
called \emph{open sets}.
def:topology-defn.statement_tex:
A \emph{topology} on a set $X$ is a collection $\tau$ of subsets of $X$
satisfying:
\begin{enumerate}[label=(\roman*)]
\item $\varnothing \in \tau$ and $X \in \tau$;
\item if $\{U_\alpha\}_{\alpha \in I}$ is any family of sets in $\tau$ (indexed
  by any set $I$), then $\bigcup_{\alpha \in I} U_\alpha \in \tau$;
\item if $U_1, \dots, U_n \in \tau$ (finitely many), then $U_1 \cap \cdots
  \cap U_n \in \tau$.
\end{enumerate}
The pair $(X, \tau)$ is a \emph{topological space}. Members of $\tau$ are
called \emph{open sets}.

thm:sigma-intersection.title: Intersection of $\sigma$-algebras is a $\sigma$-algebra
thm:sigma-intersection.statement_preview: Let $\{\mathcal{M}_\alpha\}_{\alpha \in I}$ be any family of $\sigma$-algebras on $X$ (indexed by any set $I$). Then \[ \mathcal{M} \;:=\; \bigcap_{\alpha \in I} \mathcal{M}_\alpha \] is a $\sigma$-algebra on $X$.
thm:sigma-intersection.statement_display:
Let $\{\mathcal{M}_\alpha\}_{\alpha \in I}$ be any family of $\sigma$-algebras
on $X$ (indexed by any set $I$). Then
\[
\mathcal{M} \;:=\; \bigcap_{\alpha \in I} \mathcal{M}_\alpha
\]
is a $\sigma$-algebra on $X$.
thm:sigma-intersection.statement_tex:
Let $\{\mathcal{M}_\alpha\}_{\alpha \in I}$ be any family of $\sigma$-algebras
on $X$ (indexed by any set $I$). Then
\[
\mathcal{M} \;:=\; \bigcap_{\alpha \in I} \mathcal{M}_\alpha
\]
is a $\sigma$-algebra on $X$.

def:generated-sigma.title: Generated $\sigma$-Algebra
def:generated-sigma.statement_preview: Let $\mathcal{F}$ be any collection of subsets of $X$. The \emph{$\sigma$-algebra generated by $\mathcal{F}$}, written $\sigma(\mathcal{F})$, is the smallest $\sigma$-algebra on $X$ that contains $\mathcal{F}$: \[ \sigma(\mathcal{F}) \;:=\; \bigcap\,\bigl\{\, \mathcal{M} \;\bigm|\; \mathcal{M} \text{ is a $\sigma$-algebra on } X \text{ and } \mathcal{F} \su...
def:generated-sigma.statement_display:
Let $\mathcal{F}$ be any collection of subsets of $X$. The
\emph{$\sigma$-algebra generated by $\mathcal{F}$}, written $\sigma(\mathcal{F})$,
is the smallest $\sigma$-algebra on $X$ that contains $\mathcal{F}$:
\[
\sigma(\mathcal{F})
\;:=\;
\bigcap\,\bigl\{\, \mathcal{M} \;\bigm|\;
\mathcal{M} \text{ is a $\sigma$-algebra on } X
\text{ and } \mathcal{F} \subseteq \mathcal{M} \,\bigr\}.
\]
The collection $\mathcal{F}$ is called a \emph{generating family} for
$\sigma(\mathcal{F})$.
def:generated-sigma.statement_tex:
Let $\mathcal{F}$ be any collection of subsets of $X$. The
\emph{$\sigma$-algebra generated by $\mathcal{F}$}, written $\sigma(\mathcal{F})$,
is the smallest $\sigma$-algebra on $X$ that contains $\mathcal{F}$:
\[
\sigma(\mathcal{F})
\;:=\;
\bigcap\,\bigl\{\, \mathcal{M} \;\bigm|\;
\mathcal{M} \text{ is a $\sigma$-algebra on } X
\text{ and } \mathcal{F} \subseteq \mathcal{M} \,\bigr\}.
\]
The collection $\mathcal{F}$ is called a \emph{generating family} for
$\sigma(\mathcal{F})$.

def:borel.title: Borel $\sigma$-Algebra
def:borel.statement_preview: Let $(X, \tau)$ be a topological space. The \emph{Borel $\sigma$-algebra} on $X$, written $\mathcal{B}(X)$, is the $\sigma$-algebra generated by the open sets: \[ \mathcal{B}(X) \;:=\; \sigma(\tau). \] Members of $\mathcal{B}(X)$ are called \emph{Borel sets}.
def:borel.statement_display:
Let $(X, \tau)$ be a topological space. The \emph{Borel $\sigma$-algebra}
on $X$, written $\mathcal{B}(X)$, is the $\sigma$-algebra generated by the
open sets:
\[
\mathcal{B}(X) \;:=\; \sigma(\tau).
\]
Members of $\mathcal{B}(X)$ are called \emph{Borel sets}.
def:borel.statement_tex:
Let $(X, \tau)$ be a topological space. The \emph{Borel $\sigma$-algebra}
on $X$, written $\mathcal{B}(X)$, is the $\sigma$-algebra generated by the
open sets:
\[
\mathcal{B}(X) \;:=\; \sigma(\tau).
\]
Members of $\mathcal{B}(X)$ are called \emph{Borel sets}.

ax:extensionality.title: Axiom of Extensionality
ax:extensionality.statement_preview: Two sets are equal iff they have the same elements. \[ \forall A \, \forall B \, \Bigl( A = B \iff \forall x \, (x \in A \leftrightarrow x \in B) \Bigr). \]
ax:extensionality.statement_display:
Two sets are equal iff they have the same elements.
\[
\forall A \, \forall B \,
\Bigl( A = B \iff \forall x \, (x \in A \leftrightarrow x \in B) \Bigr).
\]
ax:extensionality.statement_tex:
Two sets are equal iff they have the same elements.
\[
\forall A \, \forall B \,
\Bigl( A = B \iff \forall x \, (x \in A \leftrightarrow x \in B) \Bigr).
\]

ax:empty-set.title: Axiom of Empty Set
ax:empty-set.statement_preview: There exists a set with no elements. \[ \exists A \, \forall x \, (x \notin A). \]
ax:empty-set.statement_display:
There exists a set with no elements.
\[
\exists A \, \forall x \, (x \notin A).
\]
ax:empty-set.statement_tex:
There exists a set with no elements.
\[
\exists A \, \forall x \, (x \notin A).
\]

ax:pairing.title: Axiom of Pairing
ax:pairing.statement_preview: For any two sets, there exists a set containing exactly those two sets. \[ \forall A \, \forall B \, \exists C \, \forall x \, (x \in C \leftrightarrow (x = A \lor x = B)). \]
ax:pairing.statement_display:
For any two sets, there exists a set containing exactly those two sets.
\[
\forall A \, \forall B \, \exists C \,
\forall x \, (x \in C \leftrightarrow (x = A \lor x = B)).
\]
ax:pairing.statement_tex:
For any two sets, there exists a set containing exactly those two sets.
\[
\forall A \, \forall B \, \exists C \,
\forall x \, (x \in C \leftrightarrow (x = A \lor x = B)).
\]

ax:union.title: Axiom of Union
ax:union.statement_preview: For any family of sets, there exists a set containing exactly the elements of those sets. \[ \forall A \, \exists U \, \forall x \, \bigl( x \in U \leftrightarrow \exists B \, (B \in A \land x \in B) \bigr). \]
ax:union.statement_display:
For any family of sets, there exists a set containing exactly the elements of
those sets.
\[
\forall A \, \exists U \,
\forall x \,
\bigl( x \in U \leftrightarrow \exists B \, (B \in A \land x \in B) \bigr).
\]
ax:union.statement_tex:
For any family of sets, there exists a set containing exactly the elements of
those sets.
\[
\forall A \, \exists U \,
\forall x \,
\bigl( x \in U \leftrightarrow \exists B \, (B \in A \land x \in B) \bigr).
\]

ax:power-set.title: Axiom of Power Set
ax:power-set.statement_preview: For any set, there exists a set of all its subsets. \[ \forall A \, \exists P \, \forall x \, (x \in P \leftrightarrow x \subseteq A). \]
ax:power-set.statement_display:
For any set, there exists a set of all its subsets.
\[
\forall A \, \exists P \,
\forall x \, (x \in P \leftrightarrow x \subseteq A).
\]
ax:power-set.statement_tex:
For any set, there exists a set of all its subsets.
\[
\forall A \, \exists P \,
\forall x \, (x \in P \leftrightarrow x \subseteq A).
\]

ax:infinity.title: Axiom of Infinity
ax:infinity.statement_preview: There exists an infinite set. \[ \exists A \, \Bigl( \varnothing \in A \land \forall x \, (x \in A \rightarrow x \cup \{x\} \in A) \Bigr). \]
ax:infinity.statement_display:
There exists an infinite set.
\[
\exists A \,
\Bigl( \varnothing \in A \land
\forall x \, (x \in A \rightarrow x \cup \{x\} \in A) \Bigr).
\]
ax:infinity.statement_tex:
There exists an infinite set.
\[
\exists A \,
\Bigl( \varnothing \in A \land
\forall x \, (x \in A \rightarrow x \cup \{x\} \in A) \Bigr).
\]

ax:separation.title: Axiom Schema of Separation
ax:separation.statement_preview: Given a set and a property, there exists a subset containing exactly the elements satisfying that property. For any formula $\varphi(x)$, \[ \forall A \, \exists B \, \forall x \, (x \in B \leftrightarrow (x \in A \land \varphi(x))). \]
ax:separation.statement_display:
Given a set and a property, there exists a subset containing exactly the
elements satisfying that property. For any formula $\varphi(x)$,
\[
\forall A \, \exists B \,
\forall x \, (x \in B \leftrightarrow (x \in A \land \varphi(x))).
\]
ax:separation.statement_tex:
Given a set and a property, there exists a subset containing exactly the
elements satisfying that property. For any formula $\varphi(x)$,
\[
\forall A \, \exists B \,
\forall x \, (x \in B \leftrightarrow (x \in A \land \varphi(x))).
\]

ax:replacement.title: Axiom Schema of Replacement
ax:replacement.statement_preview: If $\varphi(x,y)$ defines a functional relation on a set $A$, then the image of $A$ under $\varphi$ is a set. For any formula $\varphi(x,y)$, \[ \forall A\, \Bigl( \bigl(\forall x \in A\, \exists! y\, \varphi(x,y)\bigr) \rightarrow \exists B\, \forall y\, \bigl(y \in B \leftrightarrow \exists x \in A\, \varphi(x,y)\bigr) \Bigr). \]
ax:replacement.statement_display:
If $\varphi(x,y)$ defines a functional relation on a set $A$, then the image
of $A$ under $\varphi$ is a set. For any formula $\varphi(x,y)$,
\[
\forall A\,
\Bigl(
\bigl(\forall x \in A\, \exists! y\, \varphi(x,y)\bigr)
\rightarrow
\exists B\, \forall y\,
\bigl(y \in B \leftrightarrow \exists x \in A\, \varphi(x,y)\bigr)
\Bigr).
\]
ax:replacement.statement_tex:
If $\varphi(x,y)$ defines a functional relation on a set $A$, then the image
of $A$ under $\varphi$ is a set. For any formula $\varphi(x,y)$,
\[
\forall A\,
\Bigl(
\bigl(\forall x \in A\, \exists! y\, \varphi(x,y)\bigr)
\rightarrow
\exists B\, \forall y\,
\bigl(y \in B \leftrightarrow \exists x \in A\, \varphi(x,y)\bigr)
\Bigr).
\]

ax:foundation.title: Axiom of Foundation
ax:foundation.statement_preview: Every nonempty set has an $\in$-minimal element. \[ \forall A \, \Bigl( A \neq \varnothing \rightarrow \exists x \in A \, (x \cap A = \varnothing) \Bigr). \]
ax:foundation.statement_display:
Every nonempty set has an $\in$-minimal element.
\[
\forall A \,
\Bigl( A \neq \varnothing \rightarrow
\exists x \in A \, (x \cap A = \varnothing) \Bigr).
\]
ax:foundation.statement_tex:
Every nonempty set has an $\in$-minimal element.
\[
\forall A \,
\Bigl( A \neq \varnothing \rightarrow
\exists x \in A \, (x \cap A = \varnothing) \Bigr).
\]

ax:choice.title: Axiom of Choice
ax:choice.statement_preview: For any family of nonempty sets, there exists a selection function. \[ \forall A \, \Bigl( (\forall B \in A \, (B \neq \varnothing)) \rightarrow \exists f \, \forall B \in A \, (f(B) \in B) \Bigr). \]
ax:choice.statement_display:
For any family of nonempty sets, there exists a selection function.
\[
\forall A \,
\Bigl(
(\forall B \in A \, (B \neq \varnothing))
\rightarrow
\exists f \,
\forall B \in A \, (f(B) \in B)
\Bigr).
\]
ax:choice.statement_tex:
For any family of nonempty sets, there exists a selection function.
\[
\forall A \,
\Bigl(
(\forall B \in A \, (B \neq \varnothing))
\rightarrow
\exists f \,
\forall B \in A \, (f(B) \in B)
\Bigr).
\]

def:set-membership.title: Set and Membership
def:set-membership.statement_preview: In axiomatic set theory, the notions of \emph{set} and \emph{membership} are \emph{primitive}. \begin{itemize} \item A \emph{set} is an object. \item \emph{Membership} is a binary relation, denoted by $\in$, between objects. \end{itemize} If $x$ is an object and $A$ is a set, the statement $x \in A$ is read as ``$x$ is an element of $A$.'' No definition of...
def:set-membership.statement_display:
In axiomatic set theory, the notions of \emph{set} and \emph{membership} are
\emph{primitive}.

\begin{itemize}
  \item A \emph{set} is an object.
  \item \emph{Membership} is a binary relation, denoted by $\in$, between objects.
\end{itemize}

If $x$ is an object and $A$ is a set, the statement $x \in A$ is read as
``$x$ is an element of $A$.'' No definition of ``set'' or ``$\in$'' is given
in more basic terms. Their meaning is determined entirely by the axioms
governing them.
def:set-membership.statement_tex:
In axiomatic set theory, the notions of \emph{set} and \emph{membership} are
\emph{primitive}.

\begin{itemize}
  \item A \emph{set} is an object.
  \item \emph{Membership} is a binary relation, denoted by $\in$, between objects.
\end{itemize}

If $x$ is an object and $A$ is a set, the statement $x \in A$ is read as
``$x$ is an element of $A$.'' No definition of ``set'' or ``$\in$'' is given
in more basic terms. Their meaning is determined entirely by the axioms
governing them.

thm:commutativity.title: Commutativity of Union and Intersection
thm:commutativity.statement_preview: Let $A,B$ be sets. Then \[ A \cup B = B \cup A \quad\text{and}\quad A \cap B = B \cap A. \]
thm:commutativity.statement_display:
Let $A,B$ be sets. Then
\[
A \cup B = B \cup A
\quad\text{and}\quad
A \cap B = B \cap A.
\]
thm:commutativity.statement_tex:
Let $A,B$ be sets. Then
\[
A \cup B = B \cup A
\quad\text{and}\quad
A \cap B = B \cap A.
\]

thm:associativity.title: Associativity of Union and Intersection
thm:associativity.statement_preview: Let $A,B,C$ be sets. Then \[ (A \cup B) \cup C = A \cup (B \cup C) \quad\text{and}\quad (A \cap B) \cap C = A \cap (B \cap C). \]
thm:associativity.statement_display:
Let $A,B,C$ be sets. Then
\[
(A \cup B) \cup C = A \cup (B \cup C)
\quad\text{and}\quad
(A \cap B) \cap C = A \cap (B \cap C).
\]
thm:associativity.statement_tex:
Let $A,B,C$ be sets. Then
\[
(A \cup B) \cup C = A \cup (B \cup C)
\quad\text{and}\quad
(A \cap B) \cap C = A \cap (B \cap C).
\]

thm:distributivity.title: Distributive Laws
thm:distributivity.statement_preview: Let $A,B,C$ be sets. Then \[ A \cap (B \cup C) = (A \cap B) \cup (A \cap C), \] \[ A \cup (B \cap C) = (A \cup B) \cap (A \cup C). \]
thm:distributivity.statement_display:
Let $A,B,C$ be sets. Then
\[
A \cap (B \cup C) = (A \cap B) \cup (A \cap C),
\]
\[
A \cup (B \cap C) = (A \cup B) \cap (A \cup C).
\]
thm:distributivity.statement_tex:
Let $A,B,C$ be sets. Then
\[
A \cap (B \cup C) = (A \cap B) \cup (A \cap C),
\]
\[
A \cup (B \cap C) = (A \cup B) \cap (A \cup C).
\]

thm:identity-absorption.title: Identity and Absorption Laws
thm:identity-absorption.statement_preview: Let $A,B$ be sets and $U$ a universe with $A \subseteq U$. Then \[ A \cup \varnothing = A, \qquad A \cap U = A, \] \[ A \cup (A \cap B) = A, \qquad A \cap (A \cup B) = A. \]
thm:identity-absorption.statement_display:
Let $A,B$ be sets and $U$ a universe with $A \subseteq U$. Then
\[
A \cup \varnothing = A,
\qquad
A \cap U = A,
\]
\[
A \cup (A \cap B) = A,
\qquad
A \cap (A \cup B) = A.
\]
thm:identity-absorption.statement_tex:
Let $A,B$ be sets and $U$ a universe with $A \subseteq U$. Then
\[
A \cup \varnothing = A,
\qquad
A \cap U = A,
\]
\[
A \cup (A \cap B) = A,
\qquad
A \cap (A \cup B) = A.
\]

def:empty-set.title: Empty Set
def:empty-set.statement_preview: The unique set with no elements is called the \emph{empty set} and is denoted $\varnothing$.
def:empty-set.statement_display:
The unique set with no elements is called the \emph{empty set} and is denoted
$\varnothing$.
def:empty-set.statement_tex:
The unique set with no elements is called the \emph{empty set} and is denoted
$\varnothing$.

def:subset.title: Subset
def:subset.statement_preview: Let $A$ and $B$ be sets. We say $A$ is a \emph{subset} of $B$, written $A \subseteq B$, if every element of $A$ is also an element of $B$: \[ A \subseteq B \;\;\Longleftrightarrow\;\; \forall x \, (x \in A \rightarrow x \in B). \]
def:subset.statement_display:
Let $A$ and $B$ be sets. We say $A$ is a \emph{subset} of $B$, written
$A \subseteq B$, if every element of $A$ is also an element of $B$:
\[
A \subseteq B \;\;\Longleftrightarrow\;\; \forall x \, (x \in A \rightarrow x \in B).
\]
def:subset.statement_tex:
Let $A$ and $B$ be sets. We say $A$ is a \emph{subset} of $B$, written
$A \subseteq B$, if every element of $A$ is also an element of $B$:
\[
A \subseteq B \;\;\Longleftrightarrow\;\; \forall x \, (x \in A \rightarrow x \in B).
\]

def:proper-subset.title: Proper Subset
def:proper-subset.statement_preview: $A$ is a \emph{proper subset} of $B$, written $A \subsetneq B$, if $A \subseteq B$ and $A \neq B$.
def:proper-subset.statement_display:
$A$ is a \emph{proper subset} of $B$, written $A \subsetneq B$, if
$A \subseteq B$ and $A \neq B$.
def:proper-subset.statement_tex:
$A$ is a \emph{proper subset} of $B$, written $A \subsetneq B$, if
$A \subseteq B$ and $A \neq B$.

def:set-equality.title: Set Equality
def:set-equality.statement_preview: Two sets $A$ and $B$ are \emph{equal}, written $A = B$, if they have the same elements: \[ A = B \;\;\Longleftrightarrow\;\; \forall x \, (x \in A \leftrightarrow x \in B). \] Equivalently, $A = B \iff (A \subseteq B \land B \subseteq A)$.
def:set-equality.statement_display:
Two sets $A$ and $B$ are \emph{equal}, written $A = B$, if they have the same
elements:
\[
A = B \;\;\Longleftrightarrow\;\; \forall x \, (x \in A \leftrightarrow x \in B).
\]
Equivalently, $A = B \iff (A \subseteq B \land B \subseteq A)$.
def:set-equality.statement_tex:
Two sets $A$ and $B$ are \emph{equal}, written $A = B$, if they have the same
elements:
\[
A = B \;\;\Longleftrightarrow\;\; \forall x \, (x \in A \leftrightarrow x \in B).
\]
Equivalently, $A = B \iff (A \subseteq B \land B \subseteq A)$.

def:union.title: Union
def:union.statement_preview: The \emph{union} of $A$ and $B$, denoted $A \cup B$, is the set of all elements belonging to at least one of the two sets: \[ A \cup B \;=\; \{\, x \mid x \in A \lor x \in B \,\}. \]
def:union.statement_display:
The \emph{union} of $A$ and $B$, denoted $A \cup B$, is the set of all
elements belonging to at least one of the two sets:
\[
A \cup B \;=\; \{\, x \mid x \in A \lor x \in B \,\}.
\]
def:union.statement_tex:
The \emph{union} of $A$ and $B$, denoted $A \cup B$, is the set of all
elements belonging to at least one of the two sets:
\[
A \cup B \;=\; \{\, x \mid x \in A \lor x \in B \,\}.
\]

def:intersection.title: Intersection
def:intersection.statement_preview: The \emph{intersection} of $A$ and $B$, denoted $A \cap B$, is the set of all elements common to both: \[ A \cap B \;=\; \{\, x \mid x \in A \land x \in B \,\}. \]
def:intersection.statement_display:
The \emph{intersection} of $A$ and $B$, denoted $A \cap B$, is the set of all
elements common to both:
\[
A \cap B \;=\; \{\, x \mid x \in A \land x \in B \,\}.
\]
def:intersection.statement_tex:
The \emph{intersection} of $A$ and $B$, denoted $A \cap B$, is the set of all
elements common to both:
\[
A \cap B \;=\; \{\, x \mid x \in A \land x \in B \,\}.
\]

def:set-difference.title: Set Difference
def:set-difference.statement_preview: The \emph{set difference} $A \setminus B$ is the set of elements in $A$ but not in $B$: \[ A \setminus B \;=\; \{\, x \mid x \in A \land x \notin B \,\}. \]
def:set-difference.statement_display:
The \emph{set difference} $A \setminus B$ is the set of elements in $A$ but
not in $B$:
\[
A \setminus B \;=\; \{\, x \mid x \in A \land x \notin B \,\}.
\]
def:set-difference.statement_tex:
The \emph{set difference} $A \setminus B$ is the set of elements in $A$ but
not in $B$:
\[
A \setminus B \;=\; \{\, x \mid x \in A \land x \notin B \,\}.
\]

def:sym-diff.title: Symmetric Difference
def:sym-diff.statement_preview: The \emph{symmetric difference} $A \triangle B$ is the set of elements belonging to exactly one of $A$ and $B$: \[ A \triangle B \;=\; (A \setminus B) \cup (B \setminus A) \;=\; \{\, x \mid (x \in A \lor x \in B) \land x \notin A \cap B \,\}. \]
def:sym-diff.statement_display:
The \emph{symmetric difference} $A \triangle B$ is the set of elements
belonging to exactly one of $A$ and $B$:
\[
A \triangle B
\;=\;
(A \setminus B) \cup (B \setminus A)
\;=\;
\{\, x \mid (x \in A \lor x \in B) \land x \notin A \cap B \,\}.
\]
def:sym-diff.statement_tex:
The \emph{symmetric difference} $A \triangle B$ is the set of elements
belonging to exactly one of $A$ and $B$:
\[
A \triangle B
\;=\;
(A \setminus B) \cup (B \setminus A)
\;=\;
\{\, x \mid (x \in A \lor x \in B) \land x \notin A \cap B \,\}.
\]

def:complement.title: Complement
def:complement.statement_preview: Let $U$ be a fixed universe and $A \subseteq U$. The \emph{complement} of $A$ relative to $U$, denoted $A^c$, is: \[ A^c \;=\; U \setminus A. \]
def:complement.statement_display:
Let $U$ be a fixed universe and $A \subseteq U$. The \emph{complement} of $A$
relative to $U$, denoted $A^c$, is:
\[
A^c \;=\; U \setminus A.
\]
def:complement.statement_tex:
Let $U$ be a fixed universe and $A \subseteq U$. The \emph{complement} of $A$
relative to $U$, denoted $A^c$, is:
\[
A^c \;=\; U \setminus A.
\]

def:cartesian-product.title: Cartesian Product
def:cartesian-product.statement_preview: The \emph{Cartesian product} of sets $A$ and $B$ is: \[ A \times B = \{\, (a,b) \mid a \in A \text{ and } b \in B \,\}. \]
def:cartesian-product.statement_display:
The \emph{Cartesian product} of sets $A$ and $B$ is:
\[
A \times B = \{\, (a,b) \mid a \in A \text{ and } b \in B \,\}.
\]
def:cartesian-product.statement_tex:
The \emph{Cartesian product} of sets $A$ and $B$ is:
\[
A \times B = \{\, (a,b) \mid a \in A \text{ and } b \in B \,\}.
\]

def:power-set.title: Power Set
def:power-set.statement_preview: The \emph{power set} of $A$, denoted $\mathcal{P}(A)$, is the set of all subsets of $A$: \[ \mathcal{P}(A) \;:=\; \{\, S \mid S \subseteq A \,\}. \]
def:power-set.statement_display:
The \emph{power set} of $A$, denoted $\mathcal{P}(A)$, is the set of all
subsets of $A$:
\[
\mathcal{P}(A) \;:=\; \{\, S \mid S \subseteq A \,\}.
\]
def:power-set.statement_tex:
The \emph{power set} of $A$, denoted $\mathcal{P}(A)$, is the set of all
subsets of $A$:
\[
\mathcal{P}(A) \;:=\; \{\, S \mid S \subseteq A \,\}.
\]

thm:de-morgan.title: De Morgan's Laws
thm:de-morgan.statement_preview: Let $U$ be a universe and $A,B \subseteq U$. Then \[ (A \cup B)^c = A^c \cap B^c \quad\text{and}\quad (A \cap B)^c = A^c \cup B^c. \]
thm:de-morgan.statement_display:
Let $U$ be a universe and $A,B \subseteq U$. Then
\[
(A \cup B)^c = A^c \cap B^c
\quad\text{and}\quad
(A \cap B)^c = A^c \cup B^c.
\]
thm:de-morgan.statement_tex:
Let $U$ be a universe and $A,B \subseteq U$. Then
\[
(A \cup B)^c = A^c \cap B^c
\quad\text{and}\quad
(A \cap B)^c = A^c \cup B^c.
\]

def:set-duality.title: Set Duality
def:set-duality.statement_preview: Two set-theoretic expressions over a fixed universe $U$ are \emph{dual} if one is obtained from the other by simultaneously replacing $\cup \leftrightarrow \cap$ and $\varnothing \leftrightarrow U$, with complements unchanged.
def:set-duality.statement_display:
Two set-theoretic expressions over a fixed universe $U$ are \emph{dual} if one
is obtained from the other by simultaneously replacing
$\cup \leftrightarrow \cap$ and $\varnothing \leftrightarrow U$, with
complements unchanged.
def:set-duality.statement_tex:
Two set-theoretic expressions over a fixed universe $U$ are \emph{dual} if one
is obtained from the other by simultaneously replacing
$\cup \leftrightarrow \cap$ and $\varnothing \leftrightarrow U$, with
complements unchanged.

cor:set-duality.title: Set Duality
cor:set-duality.statement_preview: Any identity involving $\cup$, $\cap$, $\varnothing$, and $U$ that holds for all subsets of a universe remains valid when each operation and constant is replaced by its dual.
cor:set-duality.statement_display:
Any identity involving $\cup$, $\cap$, $\varnothing$, and $U$ that holds for
all subsets of a universe remains valid when each operation and constant is
replaced by its dual.
cor:set-duality.statement_tex:
Any identity involving $\cup$, $\cap$, $\varnothing$, and $U$ that holds for
all subsets of a universe remains valid when each operation and constant is
replaced by its dual.

thm:general-associativity.title: General Associativity
thm:general-associativity.statement_preview: If $O$ is associative, then every bracketing of $x_1\mathbin{O}\cdots\mathbin{O}x_n$ has the same value.
thm:general-associativity.statement_display:
If $O$ is associative, then every bracketing of
$x_1\mathbin{O}\cdots\mathbin{O}x_n$ has the same value.
thm:general-associativity.statement_tex:
If $O$ is associative, then every bracketing of
$x_1\mathbin{O}\cdots\mathbin{O}x_n$ has the same value.

thm:general-commutativity.title: General Commutativity
thm:general-commutativity.statement_preview: If $O$ is associative and commutative, then any finite reordering of $x_1\mathbin{O}\cdots\mathbin{O}x_n$ has the same value.
thm:general-commutativity.statement_display:
If $O$ is associative and commutative, then any finite reordering of
$x_1\mathbin{O}\cdots\mathbin{O}x_n$ has the same value.
thm:general-commutativity.statement_tex:
If $O$ is associative and commutative, then any finite reordering of
$x_1\mathbin{O}\cdots\mathbin{O}x_n$ has the same value.

def:left-distributivity.title: Left Distributivity
def:left-distributivity.statement_preview: The operation $\otimes$ is left distributive over $\oplus$ if \[ x\otimes(y\oplus z)=(x\otimes y)\oplus(x\otimes z), \]
def:left-distributivity.statement_display:
The operation $\otimes$ is left distributive over $\oplus$ if
\[
x\otimes(y\oplus z)=(x\otimes y)\oplus(x\otimes z),
\]
def:left-distributivity.statement_tex:
The operation $\otimes$ is left distributive over $\oplus$ if
\[
x\otimes(y\oplus z)=(x\otimes y)\oplus(x\otimes z),
\]

def:right-distributivity.title: Right Distributivity
def:right-distributivity.statement_preview: The operation $\otimes$ is right distributive over $\oplus$ if \[ (y\oplus z)\otimes x=(y\otimes x)\oplus(z\otimes x), \]
def:right-distributivity.statement_display:
The operation $\otimes$ is right distributive over $\oplus$ if
\[
(y\oplus z)\otimes x=(y\otimes x)\oplus(z\otimes x),
\]
def:right-distributivity.statement_tex:
The operation $\otimes$ is right distributive over $\oplus$ if
\[
(y\oplus z)\otimes x=(y\otimes x)\oplus(z\otimes x),
\]

def:two-sided-distributivity.title: Two-Sided Distributivity
def:two-sided-distributivity.statement_preview: The operation $\otimes$ is two-sided distributive over $\oplus$ if it is both left distributive and right distributive.
def:two-sided-distributivity.statement_display:
The operation $\otimes$ is two-sided distributive over $\oplus$ if it is both
left distributive and right distributive.
def:two-sided-distributivity.statement_tex:
The operation $\otimes$ is two-sided distributive over $\oplus$ if it is both
left distributive and right distributive.

def:left-cancellation.title: Left Cancellation
def:left-cancellation.statement_preview: An operation has left cancellation if \[ x\mathbin{O}y=x\mathbin{O}z \Longrightarrow y=z, \]
def:left-cancellation.statement_display:
An operation has left cancellation if
\[
x\mathbin{O}y=x\mathbin{O}z \Longrightarrow y=z,
\]
def:left-cancellation.statement_tex:
An operation has left cancellation if
\[
x\mathbin{O}y=x\mathbin{O}z \Longrightarrow y=z,
\]

def:right-cancellation.title: Right Cancellation
def:right-cancellation.statement_preview: An operation has right cancellation if \[ y\mathbin{O}x=z\mathbin{O}x \Longrightarrow y=z. \]
def:right-cancellation.statement_display:
An operation has right cancellation if
\[
y\mathbin{O}x=z\mathbin{O}x \Longrightarrow y=z.
\]
def:right-cancellation.statement_tex:
An operation has right cancellation if
\[
y\mathbin{O}x=z\mathbin{O}x \Longrightarrow y=z.
\]

def:two-sided-cancellation.title: Two-Sided Cancellation
def:two-sided-cancellation.statement_preview: An operation has two-sided cancellation if it has both left cancellation and right cancellation.
def:two-sided-cancellation.statement_display:
An operation has two-sided cancellation if it has both left cancellation and
right cancellation.
def:two-sided-cancellation.statement_tex:
An operation has two-sided cancellation if it has both left cancellation and
right cancellation.

prop:invertibility-implies-left-cancella.title: Invertibility Implies Left Cancellation
prop:invertibility-implies-left-cancella.statement_preview: In an associative operation with identity, invertible elements satisfy left cancellation.
prop:invertibility-implies-left-cancella.statement_display:
In an associative operation with identity, invertible elements satisfy left
cancellation.
prop:invertibility-implies-left-cancella.statement_tex:
In an associative operation with identity, invertible elements satisfy left
cancellation.

prop:invertibility-implies-cancellation.title: Invertibility Implies Cancellation
prop:invertibility-implies-cancellation.statement_preview: In an associative operation with identity, invertible elements satisfy two-sided cancellation.
prop:invertibility-implies-cancellation.statement_display:
In an associative operation with identity, invertible elements satisfy
two-sided cancellation.
prop:invertibility-implies-cancellation.statement_tex:
In an associative operation with identity, invertible elements satisfy
two-sided cancellation.

prop:invertibility-implies-right-cancell.title: Invertibility Implies Right Cancellation
prop:invertibility-implies-right-cancell.statement_preview: In an associative operation with identity, invertible elements satisfy right cancellation.
prop:invertibility-implies-right-cancell.statement_display:
In an associative operation with identity, invertible elements satisfy right
cancellation.
prop:invertibility-implies-right-cancell.statement_tex:
In an associative operation with identity, invertible elements satisfy right
cancellation.

def:semigroup.title: Semigroup
def:semigroup.statement_preview: A \emph{semigroup} is a domain with a closed associative binary operation.
def:semigroup.statement_display: A \emph{semigroup} is a domain with a closed associative binary operation.
def:semigroup.statement_tex: A \emph{semigroup} is a domain with a closed associative binary operation.

def:monoid.title: Monoid
def:monoid.statement_preview: A \emph{monoid} is a semigroup with a two-sided identity element.
def:monoid.statement_display: A \emph{monoid} is a semigroup with a two-sided identity element.
def:monoid.statement_tex: A \emph{monoid} is a semigroup with a two-sided identity element.

def:commutative-monoid.title: Commutative Monoid
def:commutative-monoid.statement_preview: A \emph{commutative monoid} is a monoid whose operation is commutative.
def:commutative-monoid.statement_display: A \emph{commutative monoid} is a monoid whose operation is commutative.
def:commutative-monoid.statement_tex: A \emph{commutative monoid} is a monoid whose operation is commutative.

def:group.title: Group
def:group.statement_preview: A \emph{group} is a monoid in which every element has a two-sided inverse.
def:group.statement_display: A \emph{group} is a monoid in which every element has a two-sided inverse.
def:group.statement_tex: A \emph{group} is a monoid in which every element has a two-sided inverse.

def:abelian-group.title: Abelian Group
def:abelian-group.statement_preview: An \emph{abelian group} is a group whose operation is commutative.
def:abelian-group.statement_display: An \emph{abelian group} is a group whose operation is commutative.
def:abelian-group.statement_tex: An \emph{abelian group} is a group whose operation is commutative.

def:semiring.title: Semiring
def:semiring.statement_preview: A \emph{semiring} is a domain with addition and multiplication such that addition is a commutative monoid, multiplication is a monoid, multiplication distributes over addition, and the additive identity is absorbing for multiplication.
def:semiring.statement_display:
A \emph{semiring} is a domain with addition and multiplication such that
addition is a commutative monoid, multiplication is a monoid, multiplication
distributes over addition, and the additive identity is absorbing for
multiplication.
def:semiring.statement_tex:
A \emph{semiring} is a domain with addition and multiplication such that
addition is a commutative monoid, multiplication is a monoid, multiplication
distributes over addition, and the additive identity is absorbing for
multiplication.

def:ring.title: Ring
def:ring.statement_preview: A \emph{ring} is a domain whose additive structure is an abelian group, whose multiplicative structure is a monoid, and whose multiplication distributes over addition.
def:ring.statement_display:
A \emph{ring} is a domain whose additive structure is an abelian group, whose
multiplicative structure is a monoid, and whose multiplication distributes
over addition.
def:ring.statement_tex:
A \emph{ring} is a domain whose additive structure is an abelian group, whose
multiplicative structure is a monoid, and whose multiplication distributes
over addition.

def:commutative-ring.title: Commutative Ring
def:commutative-ring.statement_preview: A \emph{commutative ring} is a ring whose multiplication is commutative.
def:commutative-ring.statement_display: A \emph{commutative ring} is a ring whose multiplication is commutative.
def:commutative-ring.statement_tex: A \emph{commutative ring} is a ring whose multiplication is commutative.

def:abstract-integral-domain.title: Integral Domain
def:abstract-integral-domain.statement_preview: An \emph{integral domain} is a commutative ring with $0\ne1$ and no zero divisors.
def:abstract-integral-domain.statement_display:
An \emph{integral domain} is a commutative ring with $0\ne1$ and no zero
divisors.
def:abstract-integral-domain.statement_tex:
An \emph{integral domain} is a commutative ring with $0\ne1$ and no zero
divisors.

def:abstract-field.title: Field
def:abstract-field.statement_preview: A \emph{field} is a commutative ring in which every nonzero element has a multiplicative inverse.
def:abstract-field.statement_display:
A \emph{field} is a commutative ring in which every nonzero element has a
multiplicative inverse.
def:abstract-field.statement_tex:
A \emph{field} is a commutative ring in which every nonzero element has a
multiplicative inverse.

def:abstract-ordered-field.title: Ordered Field
def:abstract-ordered-field.statement_preview: An \emph{ordered field} is a field equipped with a total order compatible with addition and multiplication by positive elements.
def:abstract-ordered-field.statement_display:
An \emph{ordered field} is a field equipped with a total order compatible with
addition and multiplication by positive elements.
def:abstract-ordered-field.statement_tex:
An \emph{ordered field} is a field equipped with a total order compatible with
addition and multiplication by positive elements.

ax:ordered-field-trichotomy.title: Ordered Field Trichotomy
ax:ordered-field-trichotomy.statement_preview: In an ordered field, for all elements $a$ and $b$, exactly one of the following holds: \[ a=b,\qquad a<b,\qquad b<a. \]
ax:ordered-field-trichotomy.statement_display:
In an ordered field, for all elements $a$ and $b$, exactly one of the following
holds:
\[
a=b,\qquad a<b,\qquad b<a.
\]
ax:ordered-field-trichotomy.statement_tex:
In an ordered field, for all elements $a$ and $b$, exactly one of the following
holds:
\[
a=b,\qquad a<b,\qquad b<a.
\]

ax:ordered-field-addition-law.title: Ordered Field Addition Law
ax:ordered-field-addition-law.statement_preview: In an ordered field, for all elements $a$, $b$, and $c$, \[ a<b \quad\Longleftrightarrow\quad a+c<b+c. \]
ax:ordered-field-addition-law.statement_display:
In an ordered field, for all elements $a$, $b$, and $c$,
\[
a<b \quad\Longleftrightarrow\quad a+c<b+c.
\]
ax:ordered-field-addition-law.statement_tex:
In an ordered field, for all elements $a$, $b$, and $c$,
\[
a<b \quad\Longleftrightarrow\quad a+c<b+c.
\]

ax:ordered-field-multiplication-law.title: Ordered Field Multiplication Law
ax:ordered-field-multiplication-law.statement_preview: In an ordered field, for all elements $a$, $b$, and $c$, \[ c>0 \Longrightarrow \bigl(ac<bc \Longleftrightarrow a<b\bigr), \] and \[ c<0 \Longrightarrow \bigl(ac<bc \Longleftrightarrow b<a\bigr). \] Moreover, positive elements are closed under multiplication: \[ 0<a \land 0<b \Longrightarrow 0<ab. \]
ax:ordered-field-multiplication-law.statement_display:
In an ordered field, for all elements $a$, $b$, and $c$,
\[
c>0 \Longrightarrow
\bigl(ac<bc \Longleftrightarrow a<b\bigr),
\]
and
\[
c<0 \Longrightarrow
\bigl(ac<bc \Longleftrightarrow b<a\bigr).
\]
Moreover, positive elements are closed under multiplication:
\[
0<a \land 0<b \Longrightarrow 0<ab.
\]
ax:ordered-field-multiplication-law.statement_tex:
In an ordered field, for all elements $a$, $b$, and $c$,
\[
c>0 \Longrightarrow
\bigl(ac<bc \Longleftrightarrow a<b\bigr),
\]
and
\[
c<0 \Longrightarrow
\bigl(ac<bc \Longleftrightarrow b<a\bigr).
\]
Moreover, positive elements are closed under multiplication:
\[
0<a \land 0<b \Longrightarrow 0<ab.
\]

thm:ordered-field-transitivity.title: Ordered Field Transitivity
thm:ordered-field-transitivity.statement_preview: In an ordered field, for all elements $a$, $b$, and $c$, \[ a<b \land b<c \Longrightarrow a<c. \]
thm:ordered-field-transitivity.statement_display:
In an ordered field, for all elements $a$, $b$, and $c$,
\[
a<b \land b<c \Longrightarrow a<c.
\]
thm:ordered-field-transitivity.statement_tex:
In an ordered field, for all elements $a$, $b$, and $c$,
\[
a<b \land b<c \Longrightarrow a<c.
\]

def:positive-cone.title: Positive cone
def:positive-cone.statement_preview: Let $F$ be a field. A subset $P\subset F$ is called a \emph{positive cone} if it satisfies: \begin{enumerate} \item if $a,b\in P$, then $a+b\in P$; \item if $a,b\in P$, then $ab\in P$; \item for every $a\in F$, exactly one of the following holds: \[ a\in P, \qquad a=0, \qquad -a\in P. \] \end{enumerate} Define the order relation by \[ a<b \iff b-a\in P. \]
def:positive-cone.statement_display:
Let $F$ be a field. A subset $P\subset F$ is called a \emph{positive
cone} if it satisfies:
\begin{enumerate}
  \item if $a,b\in P$, then $a+b\in P$;
  \item if $a,b\in P$, then $ab\in P$;
  \item for every $a\in F$, exactly one of the following holds:
  \[
  a\in P,
  \qquad
  a=0,
  \qquad
  -a\in P.
  \]
\end{enumerate}
Define the order relation by
\[
a<b \iff b-a\in P.
\]
def:positive-cone.statement_tex:
Let $F$ be a field. A subset $P\subset F$ is called a \emph{positive
cone} if it satisfies:
\begin{enumerate}
  \item if $a,b\in P$, then $a+b\in P$;
  \item if $a,b\in P$, then $ab\in P$;
  \item for every $a\in F$, exactly one of the following holds:
  \[
  a\in P,
  \qquad
  a=0,
  \qquad
  -a\in P.
  \]
\end{enumerate}
Define the order relation by
\[
a<b \iff b-a\in P.
\]

def:unary-arithmetic-operation.title: Unary Arithmetic Operation
def:unary-arithmetic-operation.statement_preview: Let $K$ be a domain. A \emph{unary arithmetic operation} on $K$ is a function \[ O:K\to K. \]
def:unary-arithmetic-operation.statement_display:
Let $K$ be a domain. A \emph{unary arithmetic operation} on $K$ is a function
\[
O:K\to K.
\]
def:unary-arithmetic-operation.statement_tex:
Let $K$ be a domain. A \emph{unary arithmetic operation} on $K$ is a function
\[
O:K\to K.
\]

def:binary-arithmetic-operation.title: Binary Arithmetic Operation
def:binary-arithmetic-operation.statement_preview: Let $K$ be a domain. A \emph{binary arithmetic operation} on $K$ is a function \[ O:K\times K\to K. \]
def:binary-arithmetic-operation.statement_display:
Let $K$ be a domain. A \emph{binary arithmetic operation} on $K$ is a function
\[
O:K\times K\to K.
\]
def:binary-arithmetic-operation.statement_tex:
Let $K$ be a domain. A \emph{binary arithmetic operation} on $K$ is a function
\[
O:K\times K\to K.
\]

def:unary-closure.title: Unary Closure
def:unary-closure.statement_preview: A unary operation $O$ is \emph{closed on $K$} if applying it to an element of $K$ produces an element of $K$.
def:unary-closure.statement_display:
A unary operation $O$ is \emph{closed on $K$} if applying it to an element of
$K$ produces an element of $K$.
def:unary-closure.statement_tex:
A unary operation $O$ is \emph{closed on $K$} if applying it to an element of
$K$ produces an element of $K$.

def:binary-closure.title: Binary Closure
def:binary-closure.statement_preview: A binary operation $O$ is \emph{closed on $K$} if combining two elements of $K$ produces an element of $K$.
def:binary-closure.statement_display:
A binary operation $O$ is \emph{closed on $K$} if combining two elements of
$K$ produces an element of $K$.
def:binary-closure.statement_tex:
A binary operation $O$ is \emph{closed on $K$} if combining two elements of
$K$ produces an element of $K$.

def:operation-well-definedness.title: Operation Well-Definedness
def:operation-well-definedness.statement_preview: Let $\sim$ be an equivalence relation on $A$. A binary operation $O$ respects $\sim$ if \[ a\sim a' \land b\sim b' \Longrightarrow a\mathbin{O}b \sim a'\mathbin{O}b'. \]
def:operation-well-definedness.statement_display:
Let $\sim$ be an equivalence relation on $A$. A binary operation $O$ respects
$\sim$ if
\[
a\sim a' \land b\sim b' \Longrightarrow
a\mathbin{O}b \sim a'\mathbin{O}b'.
\]
def:operation-well-definedness.statement_tex:
Let $\sim$ be an equivalence relation on $A$. A binary operation $O$ respects
$\sim$ if
\[
a\sim a' \land b\sim b' \Longrightarrow
a\mathbin{O}b \sim a'\mathbin{O}b'.
\]

lem:operation-left-respect.title: Left Respect
lem:operation-left-respect.statement_preview: If a binary operation respects $\sim$, then \[ a\sim a' \Longrightarrow a\mathbin{O}b\sim a'\mathbin{O}b. \]
lem:operation-left-respect.statement_display:
If a binary operation respects $\sim$, then
\[
a\sim a' \Longrightarrow a\mathbin{O}b\sim a'\mathbin{O}b.
\]
lem:operation-left-respect.statement_tex:
If a binary operation respects $\sim$, then
\[
a\sim a' \Longrightarrow a\mathbin{O}b\sim a'\mathbin{O}b.
\]

lem:operation-right-respect.title: Right Respect
lem:operation-right-respect.statement_preview: If a binary operation respects $\sim$, then \[ b\sim b' \Longrightarrow a\mathbin{O}b\sim a\mathbin{O}b'. \]
lem:operation-right-respect.statement_display:
If a binary operation respects $\sim$, then
\[
b\sim b' \Longrightarrow a\mathbin{O}b\sim a\mathbin{O}b'.
\]
lem:operation-right-respect.statement_tex:
If a binary operation respects $\sim$, then
\[
b\sim b' \Longrightarrow a\mathbin{O}b\sim a\mathbin{O}b'.
\]

thm:induced-operation-well-defined.title: Induced Operation
thm:induced-operation-well-defined.statement_preview: The operation \[ [a]\mathbin{\overline{O}}[b]:=[a\mathbin{O}b] \] on $A/{\sim}$ is well-defined if and only if $O$ respects $\sim$.
thm:induced-operation-well-defined.statement_display:
The operation
\[
[a]\mathbin{\overline{O}}[b]:=[a\mathbin{O}b]
\]
on $A/{\sim}$ is well-defined if and only if $O$ respects $\sim$.
thm:induced-operation-well-defined.statement_tex:
The operation
\[
[a]\mathbin{\overline{O}}[b]:=[a\mathbin{O}b]
\]
